For example a notification initiated by a Kontakt.io integration will contain a floor plan image retrieved from our Kontakt.io microservice which itself retrieves that image from Kontakt.io. Currently, only a GET request to the Kontakt.io microservice is supported. No other request types or extensions use this proxy.

tags.name: Notification Profiles
tags.description:
![fusion](/images/fusion_badge.png)

A notification profile is a convenient way of grouping common message
settings and applying it to multiple [Message Templates](#tag--Message-Templates). As an example, you may have a group of emergency related
message templates which have the same notification profile so they can
share settings such as the same icon, repetition settings,
introductory text, etc.

> ℹ️ Every message template must be assigned a notification profile.

A notification profile is composed of a name and settings. Each
setting maps directly to a _device extension_ and if a non-null object
is provided for the setting’s _value_, it will then override settings
defined globally in the extension’s _config_.

## Notification Profile Settings

Currently, the API supports settings for the following extension IDs:

Name | ID
---- | ----
InformaCast Device | e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0
Email | b6d90e60-2a40-11e5-8134-9a9e6c05013a
iOS| 839771a8-4915-11e6-8b2f-d310e58d9c03
Android | 5a181177-3f02-11e6-9617-6bb7c7eddc0e
SMS | 17c57b50-833d-11e5-b222-def38c5ff9e6
Phone Call | 0a466b30-7670-11e5-ba6e-765ae9d26291
Outbound CAP | e1b2db4b-626a-11e7-a3f9-0de43455e893
Desktop Notifier | 56d6e006-542e-11e6-ae77-e3b4cf41fce9

> ℹ️ There are technically a few more, but we have omitted them for
> brevity as they are unlikely to ever be configured.

## Attachments

Notification profile settings can also have attachments, such as
images and audio, to customize their behavior. Currently only the
Informacast Device extension supports attachments.

Attachments can only be uploaded via `multipart/form-data` requests,
using field names like:

    settings[e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0][attachments][preTone]

Here, `e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0` is the `id` of the
InformaCast device extension, and `preTone` is the `name` of one of
the attachment types it supports. (The others are `mainAudio`,
`postTone`, and `displayIcon`).

The value uploaded with such an attachment key must be the binary data
of the attachment, with the appropriate MIME type header.

In responses, except when actually downloading a single attachment
binary using the specific endpoint which supports that, summary
information about the attachments is returned with the following
structure:

Attribute | Type | Description
--------- | ---- | -----------
__id__ | String | The `id` of the attachment.
__name__ | String | The name of the attachment.
__contentType__ | String | The content type of the attachment, such as `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `audio/wav`, etc.
__metadata__ | Object | Information about the attachment provided by the codec which understands that media format.
__url__ | String | The url that can be used to fetch the binary data of the attachment.

## IP Speaker Assets
Notification profile settings can also have IP speaker assets to customize a speakers behavior. Currently only the
Informacast Device extension supports IP speaker assets.

IP speaker assets can only be uploaded via `multipart/form-data` requests,
using field names like:

    settings[e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0][ipSpeakerAssets][SP-ModelName-CapabilityLabel][file]
    settings[e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0][ipSpeakerAssets][SP-ModelName-CapabilityLabel][description]

Here, `e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0` is the `id` of the
InformaCast device extension, `SP-ModelName-CapabilityLabel` is the `tag` of
the attachment, `file` is the binary data of the asset file, and `description` describes what the attachment does.

IP speaker assets may optionally contain the following field names:

    settings[e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0][ipSpeakerAssets][SP-ModelName-CapabilityLabel][exampleImage]
    settings[e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0][ipSpeakerAssets][SP-ModelName-CapabilityLabel][exampleImageAltText]
    settings[e322d549-4e80-11e6-af2d-9baf604d14b0][ipSpeakerAssets][SP-ModelName-CapabilityLabel][authoringUrl]

Here, `exampleImage` is the binary data of the example image, `exampleImageAltText` is alternate text to be used for identifying the image, and `authoringUrl`
is the url that links to a vendors web-app.

If the `file` field is included without a value, the IP speaker asset will be deleted.
If the `exampleImage` field is included without a value, the IP speaker asset example image will be deleted.

If updating a `exampleImage`, `exampleImageAltText`, `authoringUrl`, or `description` for an existing attachment, the `file` field will not be required.

In responses, except when actually downloading a single IP speaker asset
binary using the specific endpoint which supports that, summary
information about the attachments is returned with the following
structure:

Attribute | Type   | Description
--------- |--------| -----------
__id__ | String | The `id` of the IP speaker asset.
__tag__ | String | The tag of the attachment.
__asset__ | JSON | Details about the IP speaker asset.
__exampleImage__ | JSON | Details about the IP speaker asset example image.
__authoringUrl__ | String | The url to the vendor authoring tool.
__description__ | String | Description of what the config file does

`exampleImage` and `asset` will be JSON objects structured in the following way:

Attribute | Type | Description
--------- | ---- | -----------
__fileName__ | String | The file name of the IP speaker asset or example image.
__size__ | Integer | The size of the IP speaker asset or example image in bytes.
__contentType__ | String | The content type of the IP speaker asset or example image, such as `application/zip`, `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, etc.
__url__ | String | The url that can be used to fetch the binary data of the IP speaker asset or example image.
__metadata__ | String | Information about the IP speaker asset provided by the codec which understands that media format.
__altText__ | String | The alternate text to be used for identifying the example image

`exampleImage` will also have an `altText` field that specifies the alternate text that should be displayed in clients for accessibility purposes.

tags.name: Notification Recipients
tags.description: Notification recipients provide a convenient view of all the recipient [Users](#tag--Users) of a particular [Notification](#tag--Notifications), aggregating information about the user as well as whether they have read the notification, and their response to an associated [Confirmation Request](#tag--Confirmation-Requests), if any.

tags.name: Notifications
tags.description:
Posting a notification is how a notification is sent. Once sent, a
record of the notification details remains for future reference.
Administrators can access all sent notifications through this
resource, or examine notifications sent to individual Users through
the [User Notifications](#tag--User-Notifications) resource. Ordinary users
generally only have access to their own User Notifications resource,
and to notifications they sent.

A Notification resource may include sub-resources which contain audio,
an image, and [Activities](#tag--Notification-Activities).
Activities track how users have interacted with the notification, such
as marking it read, responding to a [Confirmation Request](#tag--Confirmation-Requests), etc.

tags.name: Outbound Rules
tags.description:
Outbound Rules are attached to [Inbound Email](#tag--Inbound-Email) configurations and
determine which
message template and recipients will be used to generate a notification based on
matching criteria. The criteria consist of keywords found in either the body or
the subject of an incoming email. Note that these rules work in conjunction with
the source whitelist of the Inbound Email configuration. Matching contents must
still come from an approved source.

tags.name: Placeholder Devices
tags.description: Placeholder devices (limit: 100000)

tags.name: Policies
tags.description:
A policy describes sets of permissions that align with common roles, which can be applied to [Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups). Policies are the preferred way of assigning permissions to security groups, as they are reusable and less error-prone than manually assigning individual permissions.

## Admin Policy
There is a policy named "Admin" which provides the administrator permission set. It is preexisting and cannot be updated or deleted. It has the well-known ID `f8498db0-f46c-11ef-ac77-0800200c9a66`.

## Permission Sets

The following are the available permission sets that can be used with policies. Each has a well-known ID that can be referenced for use in the API. The given descriptions are _general_ descriptions. Non-admin permission sets allow limited privileges that are adjacent to the described role; e.g. scenarios may leverage sites, so roles for managing/sending scenarios incorporate site viewing permissions.

| Name                  | ID                                   | Description                                                                                                                                                                              |
|-----------------------|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Admin                 | fa1c0721-6bc1-11ef-a246-c706490e0000 | Provides full/administrative access (only used with preexisting admin policy).                                                                                                           |
| Alert Badge Manager   | fa1c0721-6bc1-11ef-a246-c706490e0009 | Provides permissions for managing Alert Badges.                                                                                                                                          |
| Bell Schedule Manager | fa1c0721-6bc1-11ef-a246-c706490e0007 | Provides permissions for managing bell schedules and ring lists.                                                                                                                         |
| Incident Manager      | fa1c0721-6bc1-11ef-a246-c706490e0004 | Provides permissions for managing incidents.                                                                                                                                             |
| Notification Manager  | fa1c0721-6bc1-11ef-a246-c706490e0005 | Provides permissions for managing message templates and scenarios.                                                                                                                       |
| Notification Sender   | fa1c0721-6bc1-11ef-a246-c706490e0002 | Provides permissions for sending notifications via message templates and scenarios (note: Incident Manager and Notification Manager implicitly include Notification Sender permissions). |

### Restrictions, Mutually Exclusive Permissions Sets

- Admin cannot be used/applied directly. It is only used indirectly via the preexisting admin policy.
- Notification Manager cannot be applied with Notification Sender (latter is redundant)
- Incident Manager cannot be applied with Notification Sender (latter is redundant)

### Notification Sender and Allowed Resources

When a policy including Notification Sender is applied to a security group, that security group may define [Allowed Message Templates](#tag--Security-Group-Allowed-Message-Templates) and [Allowed Scenarios](#tag--Security-Group-Allowed-Scenarios) to restrict the resources available for sending.

tags.name: Public Applications
tags.description: Provides information about third-party (marketplace) [Applications](#tag--Applications) available to be installed in your InformaCast system, including screen shots to help understand what they offer.

tags.name: Reports
tags.description:
Provides information about consumable resources available to the account associated with the access token. Currently this focuses on voice dialing and SMS messaging, but other reports will be added as more features are made available.

## Telephony Report

Provides the current telephony numbers available for this account

**Report Id** 40c2d980-5c87-11e5-836c-92a0fedb8915

### Response

The Telephony Numbers Report is an array of small maps, each with the following content:

| Attribute           | Type | Description|
|---------------------| ---- | -----------|
 | __id__              | UUID | The unique identifier of this phone number entry.|
 | __phoneNumber__     | String | The E164 formatted phone number. If `type` is "toll-free-unverified" this will have the value "pending" until the number has been verified for high-throughput calling, and it will not yet be used.|
 | __created­At__  | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | When this phone number was added to the account.|
 | __type__            | String | One of "local", "toll-free", or "toll-free-unverified".|
 | __primary__         | Boolean | A boolean showing if this number comes from the primary telephony service provider assigned to the account.|
 | __serviceProvider__ | String | The name of the underlying telephony service provider which provides access to the phone number.|

## General Counts Report

Provides general counts such as subscription sizes and usage.

**Report Id** 30a05e90-9156-11e6-b405-11f7f6be9a24

### General Counts Report Response

The General Counts Report is an object with the following keys/counts:

| Attribute                     | Type | Description|
|-------------------------------| ---- | -----------|
 | __usersCount__                | Integer | The number of users currently in the system|
 | __androidDevicesCount__       | Integer | The number of android devices currently in the system|
 | __speakersCount__             | Integer | The number of fusion speakers currently in the system|
 | __pluginsCount__              | Integer | The number of fusion plugins currently in the system|
 | __maxSpeakers__               | Integer | The max number of speakers the system is licensed for|
 | __fusionServersCount__        | Integer | The number of fusion servers currently in the system|
 | __anonymousUsersCount__       | Integer | The number of anonymous users currently in the system|
 | __applicationsCount__         | Integer | The number of applications currently in the system|
 | __idnsCount__                 | Integer | The number of fusion desktop notifiers currently in the system|
 | __lockedUsersCount__          | Integer | The number of locked users currently in the system|
 | __emailDevicesCount__         | Integer | The number of email devices currently in the system|
 | __anonymousSubscriptionSize__ | Integer | The max number of anonymous users the system is licensed for|
 | __maxFusionServers__          | Integer | The max number of fusion servers the system is licensed for|
 | __maxIdns__                   | Integer | The max number of fusion desktop notifiers the system is licensed for|
 | __maxPhones__                 | Integer | The max number of fusion phones the system is licensed for|
 | __subscriptionSize__          | Integer | The max number of users the system is licensed for|
 | __phoneCallDevicesCount__     | Integer | The number of phone call devices currently in the system|
 | __userIpPhonesCount__         | Integer | The number of fusion phones attached to users currently in the system|
 | __iosDevicesCount__           | Integer | The number of iOS devices currently in the system|
 | __smsDevicesCount__           | Integer | The number of SMS devices currently in the system|


## Users Report

Provides information about users registered with InformaCast Mobile.

**Report Id** fec17bc8-73bf-11e7-81a9-f7379b42fa1e

### Query Parameters

The content of the report can be customized by supplying the following
query parameters as part of the request:

| Parameter                | Type | Description|
|--------------------------| ---- | -----------|
 | distribution­ListIds | UUID or Array[UUID] | Return users belonging to these distribution lists.|
 | security­GroupIds    | UUID or Array[UUID] | Return users belonging to these security groups.|

If both parameters are given, the users who are present in either the
specified distribution lists or security groups will be included in
the report. If neither is given, then all users will be included in
the report.

### Users Report Response

The Users Report has the following content:

| Attribute                  | Type | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|----------------------------| ---- |-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 | devices                    | Array[Device] | The endpoint devices associated with the user. Note this is the device object below, not a standard device responce.                                                                                                                                              |
 | dnd                        | Object | Do not disturb settings for the user, if any.                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
 | distribution­Lists     | Array[DistributionList] | The distribution lists to which the user is subscribed.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
 | __email__                  | String | The user’s email address; will be missing for users whose `type` is `anonymous` or `application`.                                                                                                                                                           |
 | __id__                     | String | The `id` of the user, for efficient retrieval, manipulation, or looking up sub-resources attached to the user.                                                                                                                                                    |
 | isLocked                   | Boolean | Indicates whether the user account has been locked out.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
 | name                       | String | The user’s name.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
 | passwordReset­Required | boolean | Whether the user will be forced to set a new password on next login (available only for Providers configured to use InformaCast’s own Identity Provider for login). Note that `includeIdp=true` must be passed as a query param for this value to be shown. |
 | securityGroups             | Array[Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups) | The user’s [Security Groups](#tag--User-Security-Groups).                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

### Users Report Device Object

| Attribute                     | Type | Description|
|-------------------------------| ---- | -----------|
 | __id__                        | String | The `id` of this specific device, allowing it to be manipulated or retrieved individually.|
 | __name__                      | String | The name of the device.|
 | __type__                      | String | The type of device, such as `"apns" | "android" | "SMS" | "singlewire:email" | ...`.|
 | __disabled__                  | Boolean | Whether to skip this device when sending notifications.|
 | __deviceIdentifier__          | String | The unique identifier used to address this device, such as an email address, normalized phone number, or push notification token.|
 | __blocklistTimestamp__        | String | The ISO-8601 timestamp when the device was added to the user loader blocklist, if blocklisted. May be null if blocklisted before timestamps were tracked.|
 | __isBlocklistedInUserLoader__ | Boolean | Whether a match for this device is on the user loader blocklist, preventing it from being updated by future runs of the user loader.|

## Anonymous Users Report

Provides information about anonymous users that have signed up with
InformaCast Mobile. This may be useful to clean up users who signed up
for notification during campaigns that are no longer in use.

### HTTP Request

**Report Id** ffb9be7a-73bf-11e7-81a9-bb2acacbf0d9

### Query Parameters

The content of the report can be customized by supplying the following
query parameter as part of the request:

| Parameter                | Type | Description|
|--------------------------| ---- | -----------|
 | distribution­ListIds | UUID or Array[UUID] | Return users belonging to these distribution lists.|

If the parameter is not given, then all anonymous users will be
included in the report.

### Anonymous Users Report Response

The Anonymous Users Report has the following content:

| Attribute              | Type                          | Description|
|------------------------|-------------------------------| -----------|
 | devices                | Array[Devices](#tag--Devices) | The endpoint devices associated with the user.|
 | distribution­Lists | Array[DistributionList]       | The distribution lists to which the user is subscribed, most likely via a campaign, but an administrator may have added them to others.|
 | __email__              | String                        | The user’s email address; will be missing for users whose `type` is `anonymous` or `application`.|
 | __userId__             | String                        | The `id` of the user, for efficient retrieval, manipulation, or looking up sub-resources attached to the user.|

## Opted Out Report

This no longer returns any values, because
Message Templates no longer have an opt-out feature. Eventually the
entire report will be removed once there has been time for API clients
to be updated. Please use the `isSubscribable` feature in instead).

No longer provides any values.

**Report Id** ff695489-73bf-11e7-81a9-3557b17e8348?messageTemplateId=4f86d775-4478-11e6-906f-65d9c7017cc7

### Query Parameters

You must specify which message template you are interested in finding
opted-out users for using the following query parameter as part of the
request:

| Parameter              | Type | Description|
|------------------------| ---- | -----------|
 | message­templateId | UUID | Return users who have opted out from this message template.|

### Opted Out Report Response

The Users Report has the following content:

| Attribute | Type | Description|
|-----------| ---- | -----------|
 | __email__ | String | The user’s email address.|
 | __id__    | String | The `id` of the user, for efficient retrieval, manipulation, or looking up sub-resources attached to the user.|
 | name      | String | The user’s name.|


## Get the Notification History Report

Provides a historical summary of notifications sent, with a variety of
filtering options.

**Report Id** fffce1fb-73bf-11e7-81a9-f312ef69431f

### Query Parameters

You can filter the specific notifications you are interested in
learning about by using combinations of the following query parameters
in the request:

| Parameter               | Type | Description|
|-------------------------| ---- | -----------|
 | begin­Timestamp     | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | If present, only notifications which were sent at or after this time will be included.|
 | distribution­ListId | UUID | If present, only notifications that were sent to a current member of this distribution list will be returned. Only one of `distributionListId`, `securityGroupId`, and `userId` can be supplied.|
 | end­Timestamp       | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | If present, only notifications which were sent before or at this time will be included.|
 | filter­Conference   | boolean | If `true`, only notifications which started conference calls will be included.|
 | filter­Sms          | boolean | If `true`, only notifications which sent SMS messages will be included.|
 | filter­Telephony    | boolean | If `true`, only notifications which placed individual voice calls will be included.|
 | security­GroupId    | UUID | If present, only notifications that were sent by a current member of this security group will be returned. Only one of `distributionListId`, `securityGroupId`, and `userId` can be supplied.|
 | userId                  | UUID | If present, only notifications that were sent to this user will be returned. Only one of `distributionListId`, `securityGroupId`, and `userId` can be supplied.|

### Notification History Response

The Notification History Report has the following content:

| Attribute               | Type | Description                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|-------------------------| ---- |------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 | activity­Counts     | Object | A map of activity types to counts, summarizing the notification activities associated with the notification. Their details can be retrieved using the [Notifications](#tag--Notifications) resource. |
 | __createdAt__           | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | When the notification was sent.                                                                                                                                                                |
 | __id__                  | String | The `id` of the notification, allowing more details about it and its activities to be retrieved.                                                                                               |
 | __recipient­Count__ | Integer | The number of recipients to whom the notification was sent.                                                                                                                                    |
 | sender­Name         | String | The name of the user who sent the notification.                                                                                                                                                |
 | __subject__             | String | The subject text of the notification.                                                                                                                                                          |


## Get the Notification Activity Rolling Sum Report

Provides a second-by-second (or minute-by-minute) view of the number
of notification activities of a particular type that are coming in for
a notification. This is used to provide the graphs in the live
notification view of the administrative interface.

**Report Id** 3928be41-8695-11e7-826d-bf8eb3f35a45?notificationId=22578ad8-447a-11e6-8b4b-05d1e2b39e7a&type=confirmation

### Query Parameters

You must specify which notification and activity type you are
interested in by supplying the `notificationId` and `type` parameters
in your request. You can get less fine-grained results by supplying a
`true` value for `minuteInterval`.

| Parameter           | Type | Description|
|---------------------| ---- | -----------|
 | notification­Id | UUID | Identifies the notification whose activities you would like information about.|
 | __type__            | String | Options: `set-read-status`, `confirmation` `sms-sent`, `sms-received`, `call-placed`, `call-answered`, `sms-failed`, `call-failed`, `email-queued`, `email-sent`, `email-failed`. Identifies the type of activity you are interested in.|
 | minute­Interval | Boolean | If `true`, activities will be summed at the level of minutes, otherwise they will be summed at the level of seconds.|

### Notification Activity Rolling Sum Response

The Notification History Report has the following content:

| Attribute | Type | Description|
|-----------| ---- | -----------|
 | __time__  | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | The minute (or second) at which the rolling sum is being computed.|
 | __sum__   | Integer | The total number of activities of the specified type which had been reported for the specified notification by the end of the reported time.|


## Get the Confirmation Report

Provides information about [Users](#tag--Users) who have responded to [Confirmation Requests](#tag--Confirmation-Requests) within a specified time period.

**Report Id** ff695489-73bf-11e7-81a9-3557b17e8348

### Query Parameters

| Parameter      | Type | Description|
|----------------| ---- | -----------|
 | beginTimestamp | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | If present, only responses recorded at or after this time will be included.|
 | endTimestamp   | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | If present, only responses recorded before or at this time will be included.|

### Confirmation Report Response

| Attribute                         | Type | Description                                                                                                    |
|-----------------------------------| ---- |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 | __confirmationRequestName__       | String | The name of the Confirmation Request.                                                                          |
 | __confirmationResponse__          | String | The confirmation response the User selected.                                                                   |
 | __confirmationResponseTimestamp__ | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | When the User responded to the [Confirmation Requests](#tag--Confirmation-Requests).                                         |
 | __email__                         | String | The email address of the [Users](#tag--Users).                                                               |
 | __name__                          | String | The User’s name.                                                                                         |
 | __notificationId__                | String | The ID of the [Notifications](#tag--Notifications) whose Confirmation Request the User is responding to.             |
 | __notificationSubject__           | String | The subject of the Notification whose Confirmation Request the User is responding to.                          |
 | __userId__                        | String | The `id` of the User, for efficient retrieval, manipulation, or looking up sub-resources attached to the User. |

## Get the Token Expiration Report

### Token Expiration Report Response

**Report Id** 8fda36f6-4d02-11ee-a811-79b58e8b3a00

| Attribute     | Type | Description|
|---------------| ---- | -----------|
 | __id__        | String | The `id` of the User's Token.|
 | __userId__    | String | The `id` of the User, for efficient retrieval, manipulation, or looking up sub-resources attached to the User.|
 | name          | String | The name of the token|
 | client        | String | The id of the client|
 | scope         | String | The scope of the token|
 | __expires__   | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | When the token expires|
 | notValidAfter | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | The maximum amount of time a session can be extended.|
 | user          | Object | The User object that owns the token|
 | endpoint      | Object | The Extension Endpoint object that owns the token|

tags.name: Ring Lists
tags.description:
Ring Lists (limit: 3500) represent the times and notifications that will be sent throughout the dates defined by the [Bell Schedule](#tag--Bell-Schedules) One can configure a time of day and the message template (with device group overrides or
distribution list overrides) that will be broadcasted at that time.

tags.name: Rule Actions
tags.description: Rule actions provide a way to perform an action when a specific event takes place in InformaCast. Rule Actions are a composite resource, composed of a Rule and an Action. The rule is a JSON object that follows the format of the [AWS Event Ruler](https://github.com/aws/event-ruler) library. If the rule matches an InformaCast event that has occurred then the action will fire. Currently the only supported InformaCast event to match on is initiating a Scenario Notification and the only action is sending a webhook request. Currently the only way to create a new rule action is through the [Scenario Rule Actions](#tag--Scenario-Rule-Actions) API.

tags.name: Scenario Notifications
tags.description:
Scenario Notifications is how [Scenarios](#tag--Scenarios) send notifications. This endpoint ultimately sends as a [Notification](#tag--Notifications).

## Scenario Notification Messages

Messages are associated with each individual scenario notification, and allow the sender to control which messages are sent along with the recipients to which the message will be sent.

Scenario Notification Messages do not have their own endpoint, and are instead nested inside of an array in scenario notification resources.

| Attribute                      | Type          | Description                                                                                                                           |
|--------------------------------|---------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 | __messageId__                  | String        | The `id` of the scenario notification with which the individual message is associated.                                                |
| __areaOfInterestIds__          | Array[String] | The list of ids for areas of interest that should be attached to this scenario notification.                                          |
| __collaborationGroupIds__      | Array[String] | The list of ids for collaboration groups that should be attached to this scenario notification.                                       |
| __deviceGroupIds__             | Array[String] | The list of ids for device groups (fusion only) that should be attached to this scenario notification.                                |
| __distributionListIds__        | Array[String] | The list of ids for distribution lists that should be attached to this scenario notification.                                         |
| __userIds__                    | Array[String] | The list of ids for users that should be attached to this scenario notification.                                                      |
| __additionalDeviceRecipients__ | Array[Object] | A list of additional devices that should receive this scenario notification, in the format `{"lookupValue": String, "type": String}`. |

## Scenario Notification Fields

Fields are associated with each individual scenario notification, and allow the sender manipulate the content of certain messages sent by a scenario.

Scenario Notification Fields do not have their own endpoint, and are instead nested inside of an array in scenario notification resources.

| Attribute   | Type   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|-------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 | __fieldId__ | String | The `id` of the field with which the answer is associated. Either this or `fieldVariable` is required.
 | __fieldVariable__ | String | The `variable` value of the field with which the answer is associated. Either this or `fieldId` is required.                                                                                        |
| __type__    | String | The type of field that the answer is associated with. Can be `option` or `text`. `site` is now deprecated and present for backwards compatibility with clients that have not yet been updated to use the more powerful `location` object; it will be fully removed at a future date. |
| __answer__  | String | The answer to the question indicated by the `fieldId`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

## Scenario Notification Events

Events are pieces of notifications sent by a scenario. They contain information which associates a notification and a scenario.

Scenario Notification Events do not have their own endpoint, and are instead nested inside of an array in scenario notification resources.

| Attribute                 | Type                                                    | Description                                   |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
 | __createdAt__             | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | When this scenario notification was created.  |
| __id__                    | String                                                  | The `id` of the scenario notification event.  |
| __scenarioNotifcationId__ | String                                                  | The `id` of the scenario notification.        |
| __notification__          | [Notification](#tag--Notifications)            | The notification sent as part of the scenario notification send. |
| __notificationId__        | String                                                  | The notification id associated with the event. |

tags.name: Scenario Rule Actions
tags.description:
Rule actions provide a way to perform an action when a specific event takes place in InformaCast. Rule Actions are a
composite resource, composed of a Rule and an Action. The rule is a JSON object that follows the format of the
[AWS Event Ruler](https://github.com/aws/event-ruler) library. The configured rule will match on the
[Scenario Notification](#tag--Scenario-Notifications) event object's `scenario id`. When the given
Scenario Notification is initiated it will cause the configured action to trigger.

tags.name: Scenarios
tags.description:
Scenarios allow users to press a button and send a series of notifications to separate
audiences in response to an emergency or a set of circumstances. There are several types of
scenarios:
* A standard scenario is a collection of questions (optional), message
templates, sites/site roles (optional), and an incident plan (optional) tied to a
customizable button that, when selected, initiates a series of simultaneous notifications (or
a single notification) to separate audiences and begins an incident (if an incident plan is
attached).

* A panic button scenario is a collection of message templates, a quick-dial
number (optional), scenario instructions (optional), and an incident plan (optional) tied to
a customizable button that, when selected, initiates a series of simultaneous notifications
(or a single notification) to separate audiences, starts tracking the location(s) of the user
or users who initiated the scenario in a panic button tracking event, provides those users
with a number to call and helpful instructions to follow (optional), and begins an incident
(if an incident plan is attached).

* An integration scenario is a collection of message
templates, variables, sites/site roles (optional), and an incident plan (optional) tied to
either a softkey on a Cisco IP phone for Unified CM or the act of dialing 911 on a Zoom phone
that initiates a series of simultaneous notifications (or a single notification) to separate
audiences and begins an incident (if an incident plan is attached).

Note: It's also possible to simulate a scenario to show the content of all scenario messages that would be sent without actually sending the scenario. More documentation for doing this can be found [here](#get-/v1/scenarios/-scenarioId-).

## Scenario Fields

Fields, known as Questions in the admin user interface, are objects that contain the questions asked to a dispatcher during a scenario.

Scenario Fields do not have their own endpoint, and are instead nested inside of an array in scenario resources.

| Attribute      | Type                                                     | Description                                                                                                                                                        |
|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 | __id__         | String                                                   | The `id` of the field.                                                                                                                                             |
| __scenarioId__ | String                                                   | The `id` of the associated scenario.                                                                                                                               |
| __createdAt__  | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | When this field was created.                                                                                                                                       |
| __variable__   | String                                                   | The variable that stores an answer which can be interpolated in scenario messages. 3-32 alphanumeric characters plus underscores, and it must start with a letter. |
| __type__       | String                                                   | The type of answer that should be provided. `option` gives a dropdown menu and `text` allows text entry.                                                           |
| answer         | String                                                   | Saved response to the field's variable (required when scenario is configured using hotkeys to send immediately).                                                   |
| options        | Array[String]                                            | Contains the list of options for a dropdown menu. Only necessary if the `type` is option. Up to 16 options, all of which must be at most 64 characters long.       |

During the backwards compatibility period, you may see fields of type
`site` in scenarios that used them prior to the migration to the use
of Location objects, in order to avoid breaking
integrations with systems that are still expecting to use those site
questions. Those integrations need to be updated to use `location`
instead, because this backwards compatibility feature will only be
available for a release or two.

## Scenario Messages

Messages, known as Scenario Notifications in the admin user interface, contain specific information unique to the scenario, such as a replacement subject and body and the associated message template, which is the vehicle for sending scenario notifications.

Scenario Messages do not have their own endpoint, and are instead nested inside of an array in scenario resources.

| Attribute             | Type                                                     | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|-----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| __id__                | String                                                   | The `id` of the site role's recipients.                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| __scenarioId__        | String                                                   | The `id` of the associated scenario.                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| __createdAt__         | [ISO 8601](http://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime) date/time | When this scenario message was created.                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| __messageTemplate__   | [Message Template](#tag--Message-Templates)    | The information about the message template used by the message.                                                                                                                                                                     |
| __messageTemplateId__ | String                                                   | The `id` of the message template associated with the message.                                                                                                                                                                       |
| __name__              | String                                                   | The name of the message. Used to differentiate between multiple messages attached to one scenario. Maximum of 140 characters.                                                                                                       |
| siteRoles             | Array[[Site Role](#tag--Site-Roles)]           | The site roles associated with a message. For example, a message meant for the security team might have a Security site role attached. May only be included if a field of type `site` is included in the scenario.                  |
| siteRoleIds           | Array[String]                                            | The list of ids for the site roles associated with a message. May only be included if a field of type `site` is included in the scenario.                                                                                           |
| confirmationRequest   | [Confirmation Request](#tag--Confirmation-Requests)                                                                                                                                                                                                              | The confirmation request associated with this message.                                                                                                                                                                              |
| confirmationRequestId | String                                                   | The id of the associated confirmation request.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| subject               | String                                                   | The subject of the scenario message which can override the subject of the associated message template. May include information wrapped in {{double curly brackets}} to interpolate variables from sites. Maximum of 256 characters. |
| body                  | String                                                   | The body of the scenario message which can override the body of the associated message template. May include information wrapped in {{double curly brackets}} to interpolate variables from sites. Maximum of 10000 characters.     |

### Request Parameters

| Attribute             | Type          | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|-----------------------|---------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 | __id__                | String        | The `id` of the site role's recipients.                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| __scenarioId__        | String        | The `id` of the associated scenario.                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| __messageTemplateId__ | String        | The `id` of the message template associated with the message.                                                                                                                                                                       |
| __name__              | String        | The name of the message. Used to differentiate between multiple messages attached to one scenario. Maximum of 140 characters.                                                                                                       |
| siteRoleIds           | Array[String] | The list of ids for the site roles associated with a message. May only be included if a field of type `site` is included in the scenario.                                                                                           |
| confirmationRequestId | String        | The id of the associated confirmation request.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| subject               | String        | The subject of the scenario message which can override the subject of the associated message template. May include information wrapped in {{double curly brackets}} to interpolate variables from sites. Maximum of 256 characters. |
| body                  | String        | The body of the scenario message which can override the body of the associated message template. May include information wrapped in {{double curly brackets}} to interpolate variables from sites. Maximum of 10000 characters.     |

## Location Object

The JSON object used to represent a location when creating or simulating a Scenario Notification has the following content:

| Attribute | Type   | Description                                                                                       |
|-----------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 | site      | Object | If present, contains a single attribute `id` which specifies the ID of a [Site](#tag--Sites)
 | building  | Object | If present, contains a single attribute `id` which specifies the ID of a [Building](#tag--Site-Buildings). If both are present, this must be a building that is part of `site`.
 | floor     | Object | If present, contains a single attribute `id` which specifies the ID of a [Floor](#tag--Site-Building-Floors). If all are present, this must be a floor that is part of `site` and `building`.
 | zone      | Object | If present, contains a single attribute `id` which specifies the ID of a [Zone](#tag--Site-Building-Floor-Zones). If all are present, this must be a floor that is part of `site`, `building`, and `floor`.

A full Location object specifying all four levels would have a structure shown on the right:

```json
{
  "site": { "id": "48fa0f0c-39fc-11e9-8c83-c128c081826b" },
  "building": { "id": "b321c52b-064b-11ea-9265-f333e562b36b" },
  "floor": { "id": "b321c52b-064b-11ea-9265-f333e562b36b" },
  "zone": { "id": "48fa0f0c-39fc-11e9-8c83-c128c081826b" }
}
```

As noted above in the attribute list, it is possible as a shorthand to
specify only the lowest level element that you want to specify, and
the system can figure out the parent levels. For example, if you
specify a `zone`, it can figure out the corresponding `floor`,
`building`, and `site`. If you do specify all levels, they must
actually belong in the hierarchy that you specified or a validation
error will occur. And of course, you can leave out lower levels if
they don't exist or are not relevant to the [Scenario Notification](#tag--Scenario-Notifications) being simulated or created. The
minimal requirement is that at least a `site` with its `id` must be
provided.

Additionally, to support integration with outside systems which have
their own notions of locations, but which may not be aware of the
specific `id` values used by Singlewire, when specifying each location
level you can supply a `name` key instead of `id` for that level, and
the value that you supply with that key which will be used to look up
the resource whose name exactly matches, and the corresponding `id`
will be used to build the location object.

Alternatively, if [external](#tag--Site-External-Sources) sites, buildings,
floors, or zones have been imported, their IDs can be used to set up a
location object by supplying an `externalId` key instead of `id`. The
value supplied with `externalId` is a colon-separated string, where
the first part is the `source` associated
with the external resource, and the second part is the ID by which
that resource is known in the external system from which it was
imported. If a corresponding external site, building, floor, or zone
can be found, then it will be used to build the location object (but
the `id` reported in the built object will still be the internal
Singlewire ID associated with that external resource).

If multiple keys are supplied for a level, they are consulted in
priority order. if `id` is present, it is always preferred. If there
is no `id` but `externalId` is present, then `externalId` will be
used. Finally, if neither `id` nor `externalId` was provided, `name`
will be used. If none of these three keys is provided for a level, the
location object is invalid. And if the value provided for a key does
not correspond to an existing resource, the location object is invalid
even if other keys were provided for that location level.

tags.name: Scheduled Notifications
tags.description: Scheduled Notifications (limit: 3500) provide a mechanism for configuring repeating or single event [Notification](#tag--Notifications). One can configure when a scheduled notification will fire, which message template it will use, and to whom it will be sent. You can also disable a scheduled notification if needed.

tags.name: Security Group Allowed Message Templates
tags.description: Security Group Allowed Message Templates specifies allowed [Message Templates](#tag--Message-Templates)  for the security group in conjunction with [Policies](#tag--Policies). When the Security Group has the Notification Sender permission set applied as part of its policy, only the specified message templates will be visible/available for sending.

tags.name: Security Group Allowed Scenarios
tags.description: Security Group Allowed Scenarios specifies allowed [Scenarios](#tag--Scenarios) for the security group in conjunction with [Policies](#tag--Policies). When the Security Group has the Notification Sender permission set applied as part of its policy, only the specified scenarios will be visible/available for sending.

tags.name: Security Group Members
tags.description:
A [Security Group](#tag--Security-Groups) is a convenient way of organizing a named
set of reusable permissions which can be assigned to multiple [Users](#tag--Users) in the
system. The member list is the set of users who belong to the security group, and who are
thereby implicitly granted all of its [Security Group Permissions](#tag--Security-Group-Permissions).

Every security group can be assigned to any number of users. A user can do anything which
is mentioned in the permissions of any security groups of which they are a member, as well
as any direct [User Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions) assigned to them. In Site Access mode, a security
group's permissions are only applied in the facility in which it was created (or, if the group
is in Global, the permissions apply to all facilities so long as a user has access to facilities
outside of Global).

tags.name: Security Group Permissions
tags.description:
A [Security Group](#tag--Security-Groups) is a convenient way of organizing
a named set of reusable permissions which can be assigned to multiple
[Users](#tag--Users) in the system. The [member list](#tag--Security-Group-Members)
is the set of users who belong to the
security group, and who are thereby implicitly granted all of its
permissions.

Permissions are required to perform any action with the InformaCast
Mobile API. When validating an action that a user is trying to perform
through the API, the [User Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions) assigned
directly to the user (through the access token which accompanies all
API requests) are combined with any that come from security groups to
which the user belongs.

Each permission is made up of two attributes: the `spec` which
identifies the resource being controlled, and the `verb` which
identifies the action (HTTP method) being permitted. For example, a
permission granting the ability to read a particular [User](#tag--Users)
resource would use the `spec` `/users/{userId}` and a `verb` of `get`.
To grant the ability to read every user, the `spec` would use the `*`
wildcard character rather than a specific user `id`. This same pattern
can also be applied with the verbs `post` (create), `put` (update),
and `delete`. In the case of `post` permissions, a trailing `/*` is
not required to give create access to a specific resource, because the
`id` value is not assigned until after the post action is in progress.

Every security group can be assigned to any number of users. A user
can do anything which is mentioned in the permissions of any security
groups of which they are a member, as well as any direct [User Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions)
assigned to them.

When using Site Access, a Security Group's permissions only apply to the
facility the Security Group was created in, or all facilities if the
Security Group is in Global.

> ⚠️ If the security group is marked as a "super
> group" then the actual permissions associated with it are ignored, and
> its members are automatically and implicitly granted every possible
> permission that exists within InformaCast. Environments which
> existed before the super group feature was implemented may still list
> permissions within the super group, but newer environments will list
> an empty set of permissions. In both cases, it is no longer possible
> to add or remove permissions from a super group, and the vestigial
> permissions present, if any, have no effect. When [Facilities](#tag--Facilities)
> are in use, there can be multiple super groups, each acting in different
> facilities, in order to facilitate the hierarchical delegation of administrative duties.

tags.name: Security Group SCIM Groups
tags.description:
Security Group SCIM Groups connect [Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups) to SCIM Users.
Every security group can be assigned to any number of SCIM Groups. A user can do anything which is
mentioned in the permissions of any security groups of which they are a member through their
associated SCIM Groups, as well as any direct [User Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions) assigned to them.
For more information on SCIM Users and Groups, see
[our User Guide](https://documentation.singlewire.com/fusion/en/about-cloud-user-loaders.html).

tags.name: Security Groups
tags.description:
A Security Group is a convenient way of organizing a named set of reusable
permissions which can be assigned to multiple [Users](#tag--Users) in the system.
The Security Group acts to establish the relationship between its
[Security Group Members](#tag--Security-Group-Members) and [Security Group Permissions](#tag--Security-Group-Permissions).
Each member of the group is implicitly granted all of the associated permissions.

Every Security Group can be assigned to any number of Users. A user can do anything
which is mentioned in the permissions of any Security Groups of which they are a member,
as well as any direct [User Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions) assigned to them.

A single Security Group is designated a "super group", which means that its members
implicitly have every possible permission, and will thus automatically gain new
permissions as they are developed along with new features in InformaCast Mobile.
This group is initially named "Administrators" although you are free to rename it
if you so desire. You cannot, however, delete it, nor change its `superGroup` status.

tags.name: Self Service Subscriptions
tags.description:
Self Service Subscriptions connect [Users](#tag--Users) to [Distribution Lists](#tag--Distribution-Lists) they can subscribe to. The difference between Self Service Subscriptions and [User Subscriptions](#tag--User-Subscriptions) is that Self Service Subscriptions are applied to any distribution lists that are marked as subscribable, whereas User Subscriptions can be applied to any Distribution List. While users can manage their own self-service subscriptions, only administrators can assign users to non-subscribable distribution lists.

> ℹ️  Similarly to [User Subscriptions](#tag--User-Subscriptions), it is possible to view the relationship in the opposite direction through the Distribution List Subscriptions resource.

tags.name: Session
tags.description:
Provides information about the login session associated with the access token.

This is particularly useful when your application offers an
interactive login interface, because it is the way you can obtain
information about the user who logged in. You will need, for example,
the userId value in order to look up or create other resources
associated with the user.

tags.name: Settings
tags.description:
Settings is the area where you can globally control the preferred timezones and
preferred locales.

tags.name: Site Building External Sources
tags.description:
Site Building External Sources are intended to represent an InformaCast
site building being linked to an external entity to facilitate integration
workflows, such as Zoom or Kontakt.io.

tags.name: Site Building Floor External Sources
tags.description:
Site Building Floor External Sources are intended to represent
an InformaCast site building floor being linked to an external
entity to facilitate integration workflows, such as Zoom or Kontakt.io.

tags.name: Site Building Floor Zone External Sources
tags.description:
Site Building Floor Zone External Sources are intended to represent
an InformaCast site building floor zone being linked to an external
entity to facilitate integration workflows, such as Zoom or Kontakt.io.

tags.name: Site Building Floor Zones
tags.description:
Zones provide a way to logically define a location and which recipients will be used when sending a scenario that affects the zone (limit: 500). Site Building Floor Zones are meant to increase granularity of a given
location from where a notification can originate from. Since a site building
floor can have multiple zones, pinpointing where a notification is initiated
from can improve response times in the event of an emergency.

tags.name: Site Building Floors
tags.description:
Floors provide a way to logically define a location and which recipients will be used when sending a scenario that affects the floor (limit: 100). Site Building Floors are meant to increase granularity of a given location
from where a notification can originate from. Since a site building can have
multiple floors, pinpointing where a notification is initiated from can
improve response times in the event of an emergency.

tags.name: Site Buildings
tags.description:
Buildings provide a way to logically define a location and which recipients will be used when sending a scenario that affects the building (limit: 1000). Site Buildings are meant to increase granularity of a given location from
where a notification can originate from. Since a site can have multiple
buildings, pinpointing where a notification is initiated from can improve
response times in the event of an emergency.

tags.name: Site External Sources
tags.description:
Site External Sources are intended to represent an InformaCast site
being linked to an external entity to facilitate integration workflows,
such as Zoom or Kontakt.io.

tags.name: Site Recipients
tags.description:
Site Recipients keep track of all the recipients that are associated
through [Site Roles](#tag--Site-Roles) with each [Site](#tag--Sites). The data returned here is functionally equivalent to the
data returned in the `recipients` object in the [Site](#tag--Sites) endpoint.

Note: Passing `recipients` to the [Site](#tag--Sites) endpoint
is another mechanism that can be used to manage the site roles and their
individual recipients associated with the Site.

tags.name: Site Roles
tags.description:
Site roles are meant to be used in tandem with [Sites](#tag--Sites), which help determine recipients of a
notification sent by a [Scenarios](#tag--Scenarios). They describe a group of people in an organization, such as
Security or Employees.

tags.name: Site Roles Associated Sites
tags.description:
Site roles are used with [sites](#tag--Sites) to help
determine recipients of a notification sent by a [scenario](#tag--Scenarios). Conceptually site roles describe a group of
people in an organization, such as Security or Employees. When they
are associated with a site, the specific people to be reached can be
configured through [site recipients](#tag--Site-Recipients).

This endpoint provides a way to quickly see which site roles have been
associated with sites.

tags.name: Site-Mapped Devices
tags.description: Obtain a listing of all site-mapped devices.

tags.name: Sites
tags.description:
Sites provide a way to logically define a location and which recipients will be used when sending a scenario that affects the site (limit: 500). Sites are meant to be used in tandem with [Site Roles](#tag--Site-Roles) to
determine the recipients to be used by a [Scenario](#tag--Scenarios). Sites
have recipients associated with each individual site role, which may
include [Areas of Interest](#tag--Areas-of-Interest), [Collaboration Groups](#tag--Collaboration-Groups),
[Device Groups](#tag--Device-Groups) (if in Fusion mode), [Distribution Lists](#tag--Distribution-Lists), and
[Users](#tag--Users). Based on the site chosen when a
[Notification](#tag--Notifications) is sent, recipients will be selected based on
Site Roles associated with [Scenario Notifications](#tag--Scenario-Notifications).

tags.name: Sites Management
tags.description:
Sites Management is a limited API to perform bulk operations on
[Sites](#tag--Sites), generally used with integrations to handle
imports.

While this API is public, it is relatively volatile and the structure
of the requests is subject to change.

tags.name: Supervisors
tags.description: Supervisors (limit: 100000)

tags.name: System Health Events
tags.description:
Provides access to all alarm events associated with a set of alarms.

> Alarms are a resource which receive and store information on the health of InformaCast.

>Alarm Events are a subresource of alarms. Alarm Events are triggered for each alarm type. The Alarm Event holds onto
information about what status was entered and when it was triggered.

tags.name: Text-to-Speech Defaults
tags.description: This resource allows you to view and change the default configuration for text-to-speech synthesis. Currently, the only configurable option is the default voice, but more options are expected in the future.

tags.name: Text-to-Speech Lexicon
tags.description: The lexicon allows you to substitute a word which is mispronounced by the text-to-speech voice with another word or a variation on its spelling which sounds the way you want it to. This substitution happens at send time, without end-users needing to worry about it. Lexicon substitution happens only for plain text, it is not used with custom SSML content, because the use of SSML is an expert feature that allows you to control every aspect of pronunciation and intonation.

tags.name: Text-to-Speech Previews
tags.description:
This resource allows you to access and create previews of what text-to-speech synthesis will sound like for a particular set of parameters (type, content, voice).

There are two steps involved, first creating the preview, and then using the hash returned from that operation to retrieve the actual audio data.

tags.name: Text-to-Speech Voices
tags.description: This resource allows you to list the voices that are available for text-to-speech synthesis.

tags.name: Tracking Event Activities
tags.description: Tracking events activities are created to monitor the location of individual devices under a tracking event.

tags.name: Tracking Event Notifications
tags.description:
Tracking event notifications are a way to tie a message template to a geographical location and trigger that notification when a user initiates tracking near the set geographical location.

When a device sends a response to the API indicating that tracking is being activated, notifications will be sent out to:

1. Nearby users. Each notification has a latitude/longitude location associated with it. When a device POSTs a `/tracking-events/*/activities` with `status=started` the API will find the notification closest geographically to the origin of the activity.

2. Users who appear in the notification's `distributionListIds` or `userIds` lists.

tags.name: Tracking Events
tags.description:
Tracking Events are parent resources used to associate [Tracking Event Activities](#tag--Tracking-Event-Activities). Tracking Events are created by
the service automatically if someone sends a notification and that notification has a confirmation request with at least one option
response flagged to initiate tracking. If a tracking event is created in this manner the `notificationId` will be populated.

Tracking Events can also be created by the mobile application if the user hits the 'panic button'. If a tracking event is created in this manner the `deviceId` will be populated.

Tracking Events themselves are generally not created via the mobile API. They are created automatically when either of the conditions above occur.

tags.name: Triggers
tags.description: Triggers allow an external system to be notified when changes are made to a resource within InformaCast Mobile. Each trigger is associated with a particular resource through the same kind of path-based `spec` as is used in [Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions). For example, to track changes made to a particular [User](#tag--Users) resource, you would create a trigger with the `spec` `/users/{userId}`. To be notified when changes are made to any user (including when new users are created), you would create a trigger with the wildcard character `*` standing in for the user `id`: `/users/*`. You can use multiple wildcards in a `spec` path: to be notified whenever any distribution list subscriptions are changed, you could create a trigger with the `spec` `/distribution-lists/*/user-subscriptions/*`. Currently, triggers can either perform an HTTP `POST` to an arbitrary `url`, or they can send a message to an Amazon SQS Queue. This is controlled by the trigger’s `type` attribute, which can be either `webhook` or `sqs`. ## Trigger Content When a resource monitored by a trigger (one whose path matches the trigger’s `spec` attribute) is changed, the trigger will fire, reporting the InformaCast Mobile API action which caused the change. If the trigger’s `type` is `webhook`, this will cause an HTTP `POST` to the `url` set in the trigger. If the `url` attribute includes a username and password (as specified by the standard URL format, i.e. `https://{username}:{password}@{host}/{path}`), then the HTTP request posted by the trigger will include an HTTP Basic Auth header corresponding to that username and password combination, to allow your web application to only process triggers with proper authentication. Since HTTP Basic Auth is not cryptographically secure on its own, we recommend it always be used in conjunction with SSL encryption (HTTPS). If the trigger `type` is `sqs`, then the trigger will post a message to the Amazon SQS queue with the URL that matches the trigger’s `url` attribute. > ⚠️ In order for an SQS trigger post to succeed, the owner of the queue must grant the SendMessage > permission to the AWS account 262681492495, which is used by InformaCast Mobile for posting trigger messages. In either case, the payload of the trigger (the body of the HTTP `POST` or queue message) will be an `application/json` document with the following attributes: | Attribute | Type | Description | |-----------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | __path__ | String | Identifies the path to the resource that has been changed. | | __type__ | String | Options: `post` `put` `delete`. The HTTP verb (action) which was applied to the resource identified by `path`. | | __url__ | String | The full URL at which the modified resource can be found. | No information about the new contents of the resource are reported in trigger body. The receiving application must use the normal InformaCast Mobile API to `GET` the resource in order to see its new contents. Note that the application must use an access token with sufficient permissions to access the resource in order to do that. Of course, if `type` was `delete` then the specified resource has been deleted and no longer exists.

tags.name: User Devices
tags.description: InformaCast [Users](#tag--Users) may have up to five mobile devices registered to receive [Notifications](#tag--Notifications). The process of registering (POST to user devices) such a device allows the app running on the device to tell the API that the device is associated with the logged-in user, and to provide the platform-specific identifiers needed to send push notifications to that device. Currently, InformaCast supports Apple’s APNS, Google’s GCM, and SMS for push notifications.

tags.name: User Facilities
tags.description:
Access and manage which [Facilities](#tag--Facilities) belong to which [Users](#tag--Users).
The inverse of this endpoint is [Facility Users](#tag--Facility-Users).

tags.name: User Locations
tags.description: InformaCast Mobile [Users](#tag--Users) may store up to three locations, and can then be searched for by queries based on those locations. Location-based queries use [GeoJSON Linear Rings](https://geojson.org/geojson-spec.html#linestring). For example, to find all users with locations in the polygon `[[0,0],[-90,0],[-90,45],[0,43],[0,0]]` you would append the URL-encoded version of that polygon after `location.polygon:` and use it as the `q` (search) parameter of a `GET` request to the [Users](#tag--Users) resource.

tags.name: User Notification Activities
tags.description: The user notification activities resource provides a mechanism for tracking and reviewing interactions a [User](#tag--Users) has had with a [Notification](#tag--Notifications), such as marking it read or unread, or responding to an associated [Confirmation Request](#tag--Confirmation-Requests).

tags.name: User Notifications
tags.description:
This resource provides a view of all [Notifications](#tag--Notifications)
that were sent to a particular [User](#tag--Users). The user
may never have viewed the notification, but it was sent to a
distribution list that the user was subscribed to at the time, or some
other resource that included them.

The Notification resource may include sub-resources which contain
audio, image, and [Activities](#tag--Notification-Activities). Activities track how the User has interacted with the
Notification, such as marking it read, responding to a [Confirmation Request](#tag--Confirmation-Requests), etc.

tags.name: User Permissions
tags.description:
Permissions are required to perform any action with the InformaCast
Mobile API. User Permissions are a direct assignment of a permission
to a specific [User](#tag--Users). These are often implicitly granted by
the system, for example, to allow a user to view a notification that
they sent, even if they lack the ability to see other notifications.
Manual assignment of permissions is usually done through
[Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups), which provide a way to group permissions
into a named, logical collection, which can then be tied to users.

When validating an action that a user is trying to perform through the
API, the User Permissions assigned directly to the user (through the
access token which accompanies all API requests) are combined with any
that come from security groups to which the user belongs. Each
permission is made up of two attributes: the `spec` which identifies
the resource being controlled, and the `verb` which identifies the
action (HTTP method) being permitted. For example, a permission
granting the ability to read a particular [User](#users) resource
would use the `spec` `/users/{userId}` and a `verb` of `get`. To grant
the ability to read every user, the `spec` would use the `*` wildcard
character rather than a specific user `id`. This same pattern can also
be applied with the verbs `post` (create), `put` (update), and
`delete`. In the case of `post` permissions, a trailing `/*` is not
required to give "create" access to a specific resource, because the
`id` value is not assigned until after the post action is in progress.

When [Facilities](#tag--Facilities) are in use, Users are assigned to facilities,
granting them the ability to act in those facilities. However, users may only
