acquire permissions in non-Global facilities through [Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups).
When a user is moved from Global to a non-Global facility, their user permissions,
and their Global security groups, will be removed.

## Implicit User Permissions

As noted above, in addition to explicitly granted permissions, all
users are implicitly and automatically granted a set of permissions to
enable basic use of the system. These implicit permissions are not
stored, and will not show up when listing user permissions. They are:

| Verb   | Spec                                           |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/notifications/*              |
| delete | /users/_{userId}_/notifications/*              |
| get    | /session/*                                     |
| get    | /session                                       |
| delete | /session/*                                     |
| post   | /users/_{userId}_/notifications/*/activities   |
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/notifications/*/activities/* |
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/notifications/*/image        |
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/notifications/*/audio        |
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/subscriptions/*              |
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/devices/*                    |
| delete | /users/_{userId}_/devices/*                    |
| put    | /users/_{userId}_/devices/*                    |
| post   | /users/_{userId}_/devices                      |
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/security-groups/*            |
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/user-permissions/*           |
| get    | /users/_{userId}_/locations/*                  |
| put    | /users/_{userId}_/locations/*                  |
| delete | /users/_{userId}_/locations/*                  |
| post   | /users/_{userId}_/locations                    |

tags.name: User Profile
tags.description:
The User Profile endpoint allows for greater customization of self-registered users in Self Service. This endpoint allows the User to change their name, preferred name, local timezone, and preferred language.

> ℹ️ Preferred name, local timezone, and preferred language values are only retrievable through this endpoint.

> ℹ️ Passing name through to the endpoint changes the name of the user.

tags.name: User Rostering (Roll Call) Incidents
tags.description:
Roll Call Incidents for which the requesting user is a Roll Call administrator.

> ℹ️ The name of the feature is Roll Call, though API resources relating to it are referred to as `rostering`.

tags.name: User Rostering (Roll Call) Members
tags.description:
> ⚠️ The name of the feature is Roll Call, though API resources relating to it are referred to as rostering.

User Rostering members are the users who are in the list for a particular Rostering Incident. The Rostering members will have a status and location assigned, which will be updated by Rostering administrators during the event.

Only users who are Rostering administrators for the specified Incident will be able to retrieve Rostering member data through this API.

tags.name: User Rostering (Roll Call) My List
tags.description:
> ⚠️ The name of the feature is Roll Call, though API resources relating to it are referred to as `rostering`.

Rostering My List is specific to each individual Rostering administrator. It is the list of all Rostering members whose status and/or location have been updated by that particular Rostering administrator. The user making the request determines the list of Rostering members returned.

Only users who are Rostering administrators for the specified Incident will be able to retrieve Rostering My List data through this API.

tags.name: User Rostering (Roll Call) Report
tags.description:
> ⚠️ The name of the feature is Roll Call, though API resources relating to it are referred to as `rostering`.

The Incident Rostering report contains the number of users currently assigned to each status in the Rostering event. This is used to help incident administrators with directing the incident response.

Only users who are Rostering administrators for the specified Incident will be able to retrieve Rostering report data through this API.

tags.name: User Security Groups
tags.description:
A [User](#tag--Users) may belong to any number of
[Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups), which are a convenient way of
assigning a logical set of permissions to the User. Other than
automatically-assigned [direct user permissions](#tag--User-Permissions), which grant users access to resources they create
themselves, placing Users into Security Groups is the primary method
of granting users the ability to perform actions in the system. Each
permission assigned to the associated Security Group will be
implicitly granted to the User.

tags.name: User Subscriptions
tags.description:
User Subscriptions connect [Users](#tag--Users) to [Distribution Lists](#tag--Distribution-Lists). When a Distribution List is included in the recipients of a Notification, all Users who are subscribed to that Distribution List will receive the notification, on all of their registered Devices.

> ℹ️ It is also possible to view the relationship in the opposite direction, through the Distribution List Subscriptions resource.

tags.name: User Tokens
tags.description:
Authentication tokens are sent along with every request to the InformaCast Mobile API to associate the request with a particular [user](#tag--Users), and thereby control access to API resources and actions, as detailed in the Authentication section.

The User Tokens resource manages the tokens which grant access to InformaCast Mobile. Tokens are created or refreshed automatically when users log in through the normal authentication mechanism. Your client code may also manipulate this resource directly if it needs to create application tokens to distribute to components of itself (for example, tokens for headless, unattended operation), or in order to revoke a user’s access.

tags.name: User Tracking Event Activities
tags.description: The user tracking event activities resource returns information about [Tracking Event Activities](#tag--Tracking-Event-Activities) associated with a [Tracking Event](#tag--Tracking-Events) that includes the given user

tags.name: User Tracking Events
tags.description: The user tracking events resource returns information about [Tracking Events](#tag--Tracking-Events) associated with a user

tags.name: User Update a Tracking Stream's Location
Users
tags.description: Users serve two primary roles within InformaCast Mobile. They are both the recipients of [Notifications](#tag--Notifications) (through their [Distribution List Subscriptions](#tag--Distribution-List-User-Subscriptions) and registration of [Devices](#tag--User-Devices)), and the agents of change, by logging in and obtaining access tokens, which grant access to features of the API through the user’s associations with [Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions) and [Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups).

tags.name: Wearables Installation Report Zones
tags.description: Wearables Installation Report Zones

tags.name: Wearables Installation Reports
tags.description: Wearables Installation Reports

tags.name: Webex Calling Devices
tags.description:
Webex Calling Devices represent Webex Calling phones imported into InformaCast which are able to initiate scenarios.

These devices are assigned to a site and building, and optionally a floor and room. If a Webex Calling Device initiates a scenario, the location it belongs to is used as the scenario location.

tags.name: Webex Location Rules
tags.description:
Webex Location Rules are a subresource of Webex Locations that determine the
InformaCast location of devices associated with its parent location.

tags.name: Webex Locations
tags.description:
Webex Locations are imported using the Webex Calling integration. They are linked to Webex Calling Devices
and can have rules that determine their linked devices' locations in InformaCast.

tags.name: Webex Teams Integration API
tags.description: To allow you to programmatically add the InformaCast bot to existing Cisco Webex Teams spaces, the Webex Teams extension exposes some API endpoints through its proxy interface, which can be accessed through the InformaCast Mobile REST API.

get.description: Gets a list of all Activation Groups available to the current user. (The [User Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions) and [Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups) associated with the request, through the user attached to the access token, may limit the resources returned. Controlling access to activation groups allows administrators to decide who may configure devices for fusion endpoints)

Python
cURL

curl "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/activation-groups" \
    -X POST \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
         "additionIds": [
             "CiscoPhone-388382:SEP001D45E95D12"
         ],
         "endpointIds": [
             "28a92caf-3d37-11e6-834a-431f5104df9d"
         ],
         "exclusionIds": [
             "CiscoPhone-388382:SEP001D45E95D13"
         ],
         "filterType": "OR",
         "filters": [{
                 "attribute": "Description",
                 "caseSensitive": false,
                 "comparison": "CONTAINS",
                 "complement": false,
                 "value": "John"
             }],
         "name": "Primary",
         "priority": 1}'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/applications"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'deauthorizationUri': 'https://smoke-co-safety.com/unauth',
    'displayName': 'Smoke and CO Monitoring',
    'forMarketplace': True,
    'heroImageId': '3ff898d4-1914-11ef-9d20-57fab6ce7cd6',
    'name': 'Smoke and CO Monitoring',
    'redirectUris': [
        'https://smoke-co-safety.com/auth'
    ],
    'scopes': [
        'urn:singlewire:message-templates:read',
        'urn:singlewire:session:read'
    ],
    'status': 'DRAFT',
    'testingDeauthorizationUri': 'https://smoke-co-safety.com/testing-unauth',
    'testingRedirectUris': [
        'https://smoke-co-safety.com/testing-auth'
    ],
    'type': 'GENERIC_CLIENT',
    'usePkce': False,
    'version': '0.0.1'
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'deauthorizationUri': 'https://smoke-co-safety.com/unauth',
    'displayName': 'Smoke and CO Monitoring',
    'forMarketplace': True,
    'heroImageId': '3ff898d4-1914-11ef-9d20-57fab6ce7cd6',
    'name': 'Smoke and CO Monitoring',
    'redirectUris': [
        'https://smoke-co-safety.com/auth'
    ],
    'scopes': [
        'urn:singlewire:message-templates:read',
        'urn:singlewire:session:read'
    ],
    'status': 'DRAFT',
    'testingDeauthorizationUri': 'https://smoke-co-safety.com/testing-unauth',
    'testingRedirectUris': [
        'https://smoke-co-safety.com/testing-auth'
    ],
    'type': 'GENERIC_CLIENT',
    'usePkce': False,
    'version': '0.0.1'
}
try:
    response = requests.put(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

post.description:
Creates a new draft of the specified Application. This can only be done for applications whose current `state` is `PUBLISHED`, and for which no other drafts already exist. The draft will start out with all the same information as is present in the application it is being created from, with an incremented version number. In order to perform this operation, the user must have both `POST` permission to the `/applications` resource and `GET` permission to the Application they wish to make a new draft of.
_Accessing this feature requires 'Reach'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/badges"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'appEui': '0000000000000001',
    'appKey': 'ae4e4470e68a4fbd941b77ec0018cfba',
    'blePassword': 'be4e4470e68a4fbd941b77ec0018cfbb',
    'eui': '0205f041c8e3e647',
    'isDecommissioned': True,
    'model': 'Moko_LW010CT',

}

    print(response)

x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/beacons"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'blePassword': 'be4e4470e68a4fbd941b77ec0018cfbb',
    'buildingId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-8faa-9f05022a0201',
    'floorId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-84d5-4a52e2fb2101',
    'isDecommissioned': True,
    'macAddress': 'aabbccddeeff',
    'major': 1,
    'minor': 1,
    'model': 'Moko_L01A',
    'name': 'Room 102A Beacon',
    'pinnedLocation': '{:type "Point", :coordinates [-89.50275 43.114705]}',
    'rssiAt1mDbm': 0,
    'salt': '8d6e1f0a2b3c4d5e',
    'siteId': '7e893e7c-c9f8-11ee-8be6-9f8e7d0fa83e',
    'txPowerDbm': 0,
    'type': 'IBEACON',
    'uuid': '2ac6d883-421b-11f0-8c50-f5395f75a91a',
    'zoneId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-8e64-4f8e4e3f1101'
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

post.description:
Adds an exception with the specified date range and ring list (which can be null to suppress ringing entirely) to the
specified bell schedules. It will adjust each schedule whose date ranges have any overlap with the exception to include
the new exception in the appropriate place, shortening, removing, or splitting any existing exceptions which would have
conflicted with the new one. In order to perform this operation the user must have `POST` permission to the
/bell-schedules-bulk-exceptions resource but only needs `GET` permission to the ring list and bell schedules. This
allows delegation of exception assignment without other bell schedule editing powers.

post.description:
Adds a trigger to be evaluated when a certain amount of time has elapsed since sending a [Notification](#tag--Notifications) with a [Confirmation Request](#tag--Confirmation-Requests). At that time, the responses which have been received to the confirmation request are compared with the escalation rule, and if the rule is matched, a followup notification is sent.
_Accessing this feature requires 'Respond'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/cucm-clusters"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'axlAddress': '172.30.207.49',
    'axlUser': 'axlUser',
    'capfAddress': '172.30.207.49',
    'capfPort': 3804,
    'name': 'West Cluster',
    'secureConnection': True,
    'snmpCommunity': 'ICVA',
    'snmpVersion': '2',
    'tftpAddress': '172.30.207.49',
    'tftpPort': 68
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

new copy is simply the original Distribution List's name with " (Copy)"
appended. In order to perform this operation, the user must have both
`POST` permission to the `/distribution-lists` resource and `GET` permission
to the Distribution List they wish to copy.

Note that subscribable distribution lists and distribution lists that have a campaign
attached cannot be copied.

 > ⚠️ There is an additional attribute, `userLacksSparkSession`, in the pagination response wrapper. When this attribute has a `true` value, it means the current user is not logged into Webex Teams, `false` means the current user is logged into Webex Teams. If the user is not logged in you will not be able to add and remove the InformaCast Bot from Webex Teams spaces.

201.description: HTTP status 201 will be generated if the CAP message was processed successfully and lead to notifications being sent out. The 201 response can have several instances of `<ruleset>` if the CAP alert matches the expression in more than one ruleset. Each `<ruleset>` can have more than one `<alert>` tag if the source CAP message actually contained several blocks with different tags. If the request has the header `Accept: application/json` in it, the response payload will be in JSON format.

400.description: HTTP status 400 indicates there was some sort of validation error.

204.description: HTTP status 204 will be generated if the CAP message has a tag which references a date in the past or it didn’t create any notifications. This would happen if there were no ruleset matches or the alert has already been processed.

application/xml.example:
<alert xmlns="urn:oasis:names:tc:emergency:cap:1.2">
    <identifier>Example-001</identifier>
    <sender>singlewiredoc</sender>
    <sent>2020-10-02T15:02:00-04:00</sent>
    <status>Actual</status>
    <msgType>Alert</msgType>
    <scope>Public</scope>
    <info>
        <language>en-US</language>
        <category>Safety</category>
        <event>Civil Danger Warning</event>
        <urgency>Expected</urgency>
        <severity>Severe</severity>
        <certainty>Likely</certainty>
        <eventCode>
            <valueName>SAME</valueName>
            <value>CDW</value>
        </eventCode>
        <expires>2060-10-12T23:15:00-04:00</expires>
        <senderName>Mr. CAP</senderName>
        <headline>This is a sample headline</headline>
        <description>This is a sample description</description>
        <instruction>This is a sample instruction</instruction>
        <area>
            <areaDesc>Dane County</areaDesc>
            <geocode>
                <valueName>SAME</valueName>
                <value>055025</value>
            </geocode>
        </area>
    </info>
</alert>

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to send a CAP XML payload to InformaCast Mobile so it can process the inbound CAP
rules against that payload.

The following restrictions exist:

1. The payload must be valid XML format and valid CAP payload.
1. The `<identifier>` tag is required and must be globally unique. If you submit two CAP messages, for the same provider, with the same value for `<identifier>` the second one will not be processed.
1. The `<info>` `<headline>` and `<area>` tags are required.
1. One or more of the following tags must be included: `<geocode>` `<polygon>` `<circle>`.
1. The `<expires>` tag, if included, must reference a time in the future.
1. The API access token provided must be associated with an application user with permissions to send notifications and Inbound CAP Create Event.

import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/extensions/e1b2db4b-626a-11e7-a3f9-0de43455e890/proxy/cap/event"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/xml'
}
xml_string = """<alert xmlns="urn:oasis:names:tc:emergency:cap:1.2">
                    <identifier>Example-001</identifier>
                    <sender>singlewiredoc</sender>
                    <sent>2020-10-02T15:02:00-04:00</sent>
                    <status>Actual</status>
                    <msgType>Alert</msgType>
                    <scope>Public</scope>
                    <info>
                        <language>en-US</language>
                        <category>Safety</category>
                        <event>Civil Danger Warning</event>
                        <urgency>Expected</urgency>
                        <severity>Severe</severity>
                        <certainty>Likely</certainty>
                        <eventCode>
                            <valueName>SAME</valueName>
                            <value>CDW</value>
                        </eventCode>
                        <expires>2060-10-12T23:15:00-04:00</expires>
                        <senderName>Mr. CAP</senderName>
                        <headline>This is a sample headline</headline>
                        <description>This is a sample description</description>
                        <instruction>This is a sample instruction</instruction>
                        <area>
                            <areaDesc>Dane County</areaDesc>
                            <geocode>
                                <valueName>SAME</valueName>
                                <value>055025</value>
                            </geocode>
                        </area>
                    </info>
                </alert>"""


try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=xml_string)
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

put.description:
Updates an existing Informacast extension, changing its configuration, enabling, or disabling it.

## Tied Extensions

There are two pairs of extensions which are now treated by the user
interface as a single concept: the iOS and Android push extensions are
jointly managed as InformaCast for iOS/Android, and the macOS and
Windows push extensions are jointly managed as InformaCast for
Mac/Windows. Because of this, enabling or disabling one of either pair
will automatically enable or disable the other.

> ℹ️ It is not currently possible to create or delete an extension; the
> available extensions within your environment will have already been
> created by Singlewire.

put.description:
Updates an existing Failover Pair. Note: If the backup endpoint of the failover pair is changed, the previous backup
endpoint will undergo a factory reset when the failover pair is updated. This is done to avoid unexpected behavior that
may occur when a failover backup becomes a standalone or failover primary server.

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/idps/{idpId}"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'enabled': True,
    'enforce2fa': True,
    'idpBrand': 'google',
    'isDefault': True,
    'name': 'Google',
    'selfRegistrationEnabled': False,
    'type': 'google'
}
try:
    response = requests.put(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

post.description:
Create a new Outbound Rule. Note that the recipients (users and distribution lists) will override any recipients specified in the associated message template. If no overrides are given, the values from the message template will be used. Also note that the subject and body criteria are considered “any” criteria. If any keywords are found in their respective location, the ruleset will be used to generate a notification. If multiple criteria from one ruleset match, only one notification will be sent. However, if multiple rulesets from a given Inbound Email configuration match, each ruleset will generate its own notification.
_Accessing this feature requires 'Reach'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/ip-speaker-jobs"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'action': 'VOLUME_ADJUSTMENT',
    'buildingId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-8faa-9f05022a0201',
    'floorId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-84d5-4a52e2fb2101',
    'macAddresses': [
        'aabbccddeeff',
        'ffeeddccbbaa'
    ],
    'siteId': '7e893e7c-c9f8-11ee-8be6-9f8e7d0fa83e',
    'testDuration': 5,
    'volumeAdjustment': 2,
    'zoneId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-8e64-4f8e4e3f1101'
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

`POST` request is made to the Load Requests resource, and the CSV or
Excel spreadsheet data describing the users to be loaded and their
security group and distribution list tags is submitted in the post

server has an opportunity. The current processing stage is reflected

The `id` of the attached Confirmation Request (this is what is actually stored; the expanded details about the Confirmation Request are returned as a convenience, to save clients from having to issue a separate request to look them up)
_Accessing this feature requires 'Respond'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

parameters.name: facilityId
parameters.description: The ID of the facility this message template belongs to

parameters.name: followUp
parameters.description: Denotes whether this template is usable for a follow-up: if set to `true`, this template can be used as the `messageTemplateId` when sending a new notification based on an existing notification in the notification history. If set to `false`, attempting to use this template in the same manner will result in an error. Message templates that support a follow-up are subject to different validation rules (i.e. all recipient fields can be set non-customizable in addition to specifying no recipients when saving the template)

parameters.name: fusionEnabled
parameters.description: Show only message templates whose associated Notification Profile Settings for Fusion are enabled

parameters.description: Filter list results by whether or not audio is attached

parameters.description: Filter list results by whether or not a confirmation request is attached

parameters.name: hasIncidentPlan

parameters.name: includeConfirmationRequest
parameters.description: Include an embedded copy of the confirmation request in the response

parameters.name: includeFacility

parameters.name: includeMessageTemplateCancellationIds
parameters.description: Include the list of cancellation ids for the message template in the response

parameters.name: isShared

parameters.description: The `id` of the Notification Profile used to configure the notification; omitting this value will default it to the default Notification Profile

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/message-templates"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'areaOfInterestIds': [
        '8145ebd1-7a8b-11ee-bc97-098b09a637c2'
    ],
    'areaOfInterestIdsDisplay': 'show',
    'body': 'Please stay alert for updates as we track the intruder.',
    'cancelNotificationsType': 'none',
    'collaborationGroupIds': [
        '18e34a8a-6b10-4000-889c-dd02325e5f01'
    ],
    'collaborationGroupIdsDisplay': 'show',
    'deviceGroupIds': [
        '62f123c8-e0a5-11ee-9d3d-89d4670a1082'
    ],
    'deviceGroupIdsDisplay': 'show',
    'distributionListIds': [
        '4c6e2bfb-7a8d-11ee-bc97-7f85b6d2e47c'
    ],
    'distributionListIdsDisplay': 'show',
    'imageAltText': 'Green checkmark indicating completion',
    'incidentPlanId': '36b2411b-cd0b-11ee-9027-edf5cd113c88',
    'incidentPlanIdCustomizable': True,
    'incidentPlanIdDisplay': 'show',
    'isShared': True,
    'name': 'Intruder Alert',
    'priority': 'critical',
    'subject': 'An intruder has been spotted!',
    'ttsSpeed': 'normal',
    'ttsType': 'subject-and-body',
    'ttsVoiceId': '751de14c-841a-11ea-a32f-2527bd7fe7a8',
    'userIds': [
        'ea0b5ce4-7a8b-11ee-bc97-1de018eb9fe0'
    ],
    'userIdsDisplay': 'show'
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/message-templates/{messageTemplateId}"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'areaOfInterestIds': [
        '8145ebd1-7a8b-11ee-bc97-098b09a637c2'
    ],
    'areaOfInterestIdsDisplay': 'show',
    'body': 'Please stay alert for updates as we track the intruder.',
    'cancelNotificationsType': 'none',
    'collaborationGroupIds': [
        '18e34a8a-6b10-4000-889c-dd02325e5f01'
    ],
    'collaborationGroupIdsDisplay': 'show',
    'deviceGroupIds': [
        '62f123c8-e0a5-11ee-9d3d-89d4670a1082'
    ],
    'deviceGroupIdsDisplay': 'show',
    'distributionListIds': [
        '4c6e2bfb-7a8d-11ee-bc97-7f85b6d2e47c'
    ],
    'distributionListIdsDisplay': 'show',
    'imageAltText': 'Green checkmark indicating completion',
    'incidentPlanId': '36b2411b-cd0b-11ee-9027-edf5cd113c88',
    'incidentPlanIdCustomizable': True,
    'incidentPlanIdDisplay': 'show',
    'isShared': True,
    'name': 'Intruder Alert',
    'priority': 'critical',
    'subject': 'An intruder has been spotted!',
    'ttsSpeed': 'normal',
    'ttsType': 'subject-and-body',
    'ttsVoiceId': '751de14c-841a-11ea-a32f-2527bd7fe7a8',
    'userIds': [
        'ea0b5ce4-7a8b-11ee-bc97-1de018eb9fe0'
    ],
    'userIdsDisplay': 'show'
}
try:
    response = requests.put(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

improved performance, this will likely involve redirection (via an
HTTP 302 Redirect response) to an AWS CloudFront content distribution
network node which has cached the content closer geographically to
where the request originated than the Singlewire REST API servers.

get.description:
Gets a single attachment for the notification profile settings by its `id`.

> ℹ️ Currently only the InformaCast device extension supports/honors
> attachments, with the following names and purposes:

Name | Purpose
---- | -------
mainAudio | Either the audio file to play to recipients during a pre-recorded audio message, or an activation alert that plays while devices are activating for a live audio message.
displayIcon | The image file to use as an icon (displays on compatible devices; cropped to 38x64 and positioned at the top left corner of a message’s display).
postTone | Audio that plays to recipients to designate an ending message.
preTone | Audio that plays to recipients to announce an impending message.

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/notifications"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'additionalDeviceRecipients': [
        {
            'lookupValue': '12345',
            'type': 'ip-speaker'
        }
    ],
    'areaOfInterestIds': [
        '8145ebd1-7a8b-11ee-bc97-098b09a637c2'
    ],
    'body': 'Warning: An intruder has been spotted on campus heading towards the recreation center. Please be advised and stay tuned for further updates. Have you seen the intruder?',
    'collaborationGroupIds': [
        '18e34a8a-6b10-4000-889c-dd02325e5f01'
    ],
    'confirmationRequestId': '80a5b997-e0b9-11ee-80c3-b5928b1c397d',
    'customVars': '{"affectedCounty" "Dane", "affectedState" "Wisconsin"}',
    'deviceGroupIds': [
        '62f123c8-e0a5-11ee-9d3d-89d4670a1082'
    ],
    'distributionListIds': [
        '4c6e2bfb-7a8d-11ee-bc97-7f85b6d2e47c'
    ],
    'extensionSettingsOverrides': '{"0a466b30767011e5Ba6e765ae9d26291" {:disabled true}}',
    'followUpForNotificationQuery': 'read:true',
    'imageAltText': 'Green checkmark indicating completion',
    'messageTemplateId': 'd2d02884-7ceb-11ee-9061-050e02dd61f4',
    'notificationSource': 'administration-console',
    'subject': 'An intruder has been spotted on campus',
    'ttsSpeed': 'normal',
    'ttsVoiceId': '751de14c-841a-11ea-a32f-2527bd7fe7a8',
    'userIds': [
        'ea0b5ce4-7a8b-11ee-bc97-1de018eb9fe0'
    ]
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

post.description:
Creates a notification to be sent to one or more specified recipients.
These may be individual [Users](#tag--Users) or a group of
recipients specified by [Distribution Lists](#tag--Distribution-Lists),
[Collaboration Groups](#tag--Collaboration-Groups), [Areas of Interest](#tag--Areas-of-Interest), or [Device Groups](#tag--Device-Groups) (for Fusion customers).
Notifications are always based on a [Message Template](#tag--Message-Templates), but values from the template may be overridden at
send time (as long as the template allows this). Any attributes in the
template which are required but not marked as customizable must be
present, with values matching those in the template. At least one
recipient must be specified (whether in the Message Template or the
request parameters), or the `POST` will fail validation.

administrative users will have permission to do this.

permissions.description: Which operations are possible on this resource, given the authentication token being used to access the API

updatedAt.description: When this resource was last updated

createdAt.description: The created time of the notification

user.description: The User whose interaction with the notification is being recorded

readStatus.description: Present when the type attribute is set-read-status, and will have the value read or unread. Marks the notification as read or unread as of the time of this activity. The read status can be toggled multiple times; the value set by the most recent activity is considered the current state

id.description: The id of this specific activity, allowing it to be manipulated or retrieved individually

userId.description: The id of the user whose interaction with the notification is being recorded

notificationId.description: The id of the user whose interaction with the notification is being recorded

type.description: Identifies the type of activity that was recorded. Extensions may add more type values; this is where the email-related values came from. Such extension-defined types will not be present in the API Explorer documentation, since the API is not aware of them

details.description: Typically just a comment field e.g. {"comment":"Weather alert removed by NWS"}

confirmationOption.description: When the type attribute is confirmation, this contains the Confirmation Request response the user has chosen. This will be a value from the options list of the confirmation request attached to the notification, and there will be at most one activity of this type recorded for this user and notification

post.summary: Create an Activity To Cancel Or Expire a Notification

type.description: Identifies the type of activity that was recorded. Extensions may add more type values; this is where the email-related values came from. Such extension-defined types will not be present in the API Explorer documentation, since the API is not aware of them

details.description: Typically just a comment field e.g. {"comment":"Weather alert removed by NWS"}

parameters.name: notificationId
parameters.description: The notification id path parameter

post.description: Creates a new activity based on the `type` for the specified notification. This mechanism can currently be used to cancel or expire notifications. Canceling can be attempted while a notification is actively being sent to recipients. Canceling will not delete notifications that were successfully delivered to recipients and cannot be initiated after all recipients have been notified (i.e. after the notification’s status is `complete`). You can expire notifications that have been completed and are active. This does not remove the notification from your history, nor does it delete notifications from users who have already viewed the message. It does, however, update the status of the notification to show that it has expired.

binary stream. Typically this is μ-law encoded compressed audio. For
improved performance, this will likely involve redirection (via an
HTTP 302 Redirect response) to an AWS CloudFront content distribution
network node which has cached the content closer geographically to
where the request originated than the Singlewire REST API servers.

get.description:
Gets a list of all extensions that have proxy routes available for notification data available to the current user. The ID of an extension is used in the proxy route to get notification information from that extension. For example, the ID of the Kontakt.io extension is `44d2902c-4344-11ec-bf8d-f70025e4774c`, so you would proxy a `floor-details` request to that extension with `/notifications/{notificiationId}/extensions/44d2902c-4344-11ec-bf8d-f70025e4774c/proxy/floor-details` (the [Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions) and [Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups) associated with the request, through
the user attached to the access token, may limit the resources
returned).

Retrieves the image, if any, that was sent with the notification, as a
binary stream. For improved performance, this will likely involve
redirection (via an HTTP 302 Redirect response) to an AWS CloudFront
content distribution network node which has cached the content closer
geographically to where the request originated than the Singlewire
REST API servers.

get.description: Retrieves the list of all reports available. (The permissions associated with the request, through the user attached to the access token, may limit the visibility of reports.)

parameters.name: beginTimestamp
parameters.description: **Notification History Report/Confirmation Report Only** If present, only notifications which were sent at or after this time will be included.

parameters.name: devicesDisabled
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** If included only users with/without disabled devices should be returned.

parameters.name: devicesExcluded
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** If included only users with/without excluded devices should be returned.

parameters.name: devicesUnverified
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** If included only users with/without unverified devices should be returned.

parameters.name: distributionListId
parameters.description: **Notification History Report Only** If present, only notifications that were sent to a current member of this distribution list will be returned. Only one of `distributionListId`, `securityGroupId`, and `userId` can be supplied.

parameters.name: distributionListIds
parameters.description: **Users Report/Anonymous Users Report Only** Return users belonging to these distribution lists.

parameters.name: endTimestamp
parameters.description: **Notification History Report/Confirmation Report Only** If present, only notifications which were sent before or at this time will be included.

parameters.name: expires
parameters.description: **Expiration Report Only** If present, shows tokens that are filtered on their expiration time.

parameters.name: facilityId
parameters.description: **Users Report/Notification History Report/Confirmation Report Only** If included only results belonging to the specified facility should be returned.

parameters.name: filterConference
parameters.description: **Notification History Report Only** If `true`, only notifications which started conference calls will be included.

parameters.name: filterSms
parameters.description: **Notification History Report Only** If `true`, only notifications which sent SMS messages will be included.

parameters.name: includeEmergencyBuildings
parameters.description: **General Counts Report Only** Whether or not to include emergency calling buildings in the report. Defaults to `true`.

parameters.name: includeEndpointDevices
parameters.description: **General Counts Report Only** Whether or not to include endpoint devices in the report. Defaults to `true`.

parameters.name: includeUserDevices
parameters.description: **General Counts Report Only** Whether or not to include user devices in the report. Defaults to `true`.

parameters.name: includeUsers
parameters.description: **General Counts Report Only** Whether or not to include users in the report. Defaults to `true`.

parameters.name: isLocked
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** If included only users with matching locked state should be returned.

parameters.name: messageTemplateId
parameters.description: **Opted Out Report Only** Return users who have opted out from this message template.

parameters.name: minuteInterval
parameters.description: **Notification Activity Rolling Sum Report Only** If `true`, activities will be summed at the level of minutes, otherwise they will be summed at the level of seconds.

parameters.name: notificationId
parameters.description: **Notification Activity Rolling Sum Report Only** Identifies the notification whose activities you would like information about.

parameters.name: passwordResetRequired
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** If included only users with matching password reset state should be returned.

parameters.name: receivedLastDays
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** If included only users who have received a notification in the last X days should be returned.

parameters.name: reportId
parameters.description: The report id path parameter

parameters.name: securityGroupId
parameters.description: **Notification History Report Only** If present, only notifications that were sent by a current member of this security group will be returned. Only one of `distributionListId`, `securityGroupId`, and `userId` can be supplied.

parameters.name: securityGroupIds
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** Return users belonging to these security groups.

parameters.name: sentLastDays
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** If included only users who have sent a notification in the last X days should be returned.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: **Notification Activity Rolling Sum Report Only** Identifies the type of activity you are interested in.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: **Notification History Report Only** If present, only notifications that were sent to this user will be returned. Only one of `distributionListId`, `securityGroupId`, and `userId` can be supplied.

parameters.name: userTypes
parameters.description: **Users Report Only** Return users belonging to these user types.

x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/scenarios"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'backgroundColor': '#33AA55',
    'description': 'A notification informing employees about a chemical spill, providing urgent instructions on evacuation, safety measures, and updates.',
    'fields': [{
            'name': 'Is everyone ok?',
            'rank': 1,
            'type': 'text',
            'variable': 'fieldVar1'
        }],
    'icon': 'flash',
    'instructions': {},
    'isShared': True,
    'locationEnabled': False,
    'messages': [{
            'confirmationRequestId': '80a5b997-e0b9-11ee-80c3-b5928b1c397d',
            'messageTemplateId': 'd2d02884-7ceb-11ee-9061-050e02dd61f4',
            'name': 'Live fire'
        }],
    'name': 'Chemical Spill',
    'type': ':standard'
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

get.description:
Retrieves a single scenario by `id`. Since this is not a list request, there is no pagination wrapper around the result. ## Simulate Answers It is also possible to Simulate Answers by passing query parameters `simulatedAnswers` and optionally `location` (if the scenario is location-enabled). This will build a simulated scenario which has the contents of all scenario messages that would be sent if the scenario is sent with those answers and location choices. This is useful for previewing [Scenario Notifications](#tag--Scenario-Notifications), since it will interpolate variables from questions and location and join the recipients from sites/site roles and message templates. If the scenario is an emergency scenario, it is also possible when simulating answers to pass the query parameter `checkPanicRange` to test whether a particular latitude and longitude is within a mile from any emergency building. Such a query will return a `withinPanicRange` boolean attribute in the response, indicating whether launching an emergency mobile panic button scenario from that location will automatically contact emergency services. The value sent as `checkPanicRange` will also be returned as `position`. ### Request Parameters | Parameter | Type | Description | |----------------------|---------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | check­PanicRange | Object | If the scenario is an emergency scenario, this can hold an object in the format `{"latitude": Number, "longitude": Number}` to check whether a mobile panic button launched from that geographic position can automatically contact emergency services. | | location | Object | If the scenario is location-enabled, a [Location Object](#tag--Scenarios) that specifies the site, building, floor, and/or zone to associate with the scenario. | | simulatedAnswers | Array[Object] | An array of objects in the format `{"fieldId": UUID, "answer": String}`. These are the answers to any questions attached to the scenario. |
_Accessing this feature requires 'Reach'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

post.description:
Creates copies of a Security Group in either all or a subset of the [Facilities](#tag--Facilities) the user can access.
The name of each copy is simply the original Security Group's name with the
appropriate facility name prefixed.
Each copy only maintains the global permissions of the original group.
In order to perform this operation, the user must have `POST` permission to the `/security-groups` resource, `GET` permission
to the Security Group they wish to copy, and access to all requested facility ids.

post.description:
Grants a group the permission to perform a specific action on a specified resource or list of resources.
### Tied Extensions

There are two pairs of extensions which are now treated by the user
interface as a single concept: the iOS and Android push extensions are
jointly managed as InformaCast for iOS/Android, and the macOS and
Windows push extensions are jointly managed as InformaCast for
Mac/Windows. Because of this, granting a permission to one of either
pair will automatically grant permission to the other.

Updates an existing security group permission, to change the `spec` or the `verb`, as needed.

### Tied Extensions

There are two pairs of extensions which are now treated by the user
interface as a single concept: the iOS and Android push extensions are
jointly managed as InformaCast for iOS/Android, and the macOS and
Windows push extensions are jointly managed as InformaCast for
Mac/Windows. Because of this, granting or revoking a permission to one
of either pair will automatically grant or revoke permission to the
other.

### Tied Extensions

There are two pairs of extensions which are now treated by the user
interface as a single concept: the iOS and Android push extensions are
jointly managed as InformaCast for iOS/Android, and the macOS and
Windows push extensions are jointly managed as InformaCast for
Mac/Windows. Because of this, revoking a permission to one of either
pair will automatically revoke permission to the other.

get.description:
Retrieves the authentication session information associated with the
access token, as well as information about the system configuration.
This is not a paginated result.

### Session Features

The `features` attribute can contain the following values:

Name | Description
--------- | -----------
applePush | Represents that the Apple push extension is enabled and Apple devices can be registered and push notifications placed
conferenceCall | Represents that the conference call extension is enabled and conference call collaboration groups can be used
cucmEnabled | Represents that integration with Cisco Unified Communications Manager is enabled
demo | Represents that the provider's account is in demo mode
desktopNotifier | Represents that the desktop notifier extension is enabled and desktop notifier devices can be registered and sent notifications
dnd | Represents that the do not disturb extension is enabled and can be used
email | Represents that the email extension is enabled and email devices can be registered and sent messages to
facebook | Represents that the Facebook extension is enabled
failover | Represents that failover mode is enabled
firebasePush | Represents that the Android/Firebase extension is enabled and Android/Firebase devices can be registered and push notifications placed
fusion | Represents that fusion mode is enabled
gms | Represents that GMS mode is enabled
inboundCap | Represents that the inbound CAP extension is enabled
inboundRss | Represents that the inbound RSS extension is enabled and can be used
msteamsCall | Represents the Microsoft Teams call extension is enabled
msteamsChat | Represents the Microsoft Teams chat extension is enabled
openApi | Indicates that OpenAPI documentation is available
outboundCap | Represents that the outbound CAP extension is enabled
outboundCapDevice | Represents that the outbound CAP device extension is enabled
phoneCall | Represents that the phone call extension is enabled and phone devices can be registered and calls placed
reporting | Represents that the reporting extension is enabled and the reporting extension can be used
sms | Represents that the SMS extension is enabled and SMS devices can be registered and sent messages to
webexTeams | Represents that the Cisco Webex Teams extension is enabled

    -X DELETE \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND="

    -H "Authorization: Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
         "action": "unlink-sources",
         "externalSites": "[{\"name\": \"external-site\", \"source\": \"kontaktio-wearable-panic-button\", \"externalId\": \"e5ca6e72-018d-11ef-9a79-f70ca05b7fe9\"}]",
         "sources": "[\"kontaktio-wearable-panic-button\"]"}'

post.description:
Updates the state of a Sites integration. Depending on the value of
`action`, performs one of the following four operations:

### Unlink Sources

Using an `action` of `unlink-sources` unlinks all sites with a source
listed in `sources`. Removes the `externalSource` record for the
sites. Does not delete or modify the sites in any other way. Additionally, in
a future release, specific sites for the given `sources` can be unlinked
by supplying them directly in `externalSites`.

### Import

Using an action of `import` imports the sites supplied in
`externalSites`. Note that this is in a very particular format that we
only expect to be used via the Singlewire administrative web
application.

### Import by External ID

In a future release, using an action of `import-by-external-id` imports the sites
supplied in `externalSites` and links them directly to existing InformaCast sites
and buildings by id. Similarly to the `import` action, this will use a very
particular format only expected to be used via the Singlewire administrative web
application.

### Update by External ID

In a future release, using an action of `update-by-external-id` updates the sites supplied
in `externalSites` using the same link established by the `import-by-external-id` action.
Similarly to the `import-by-external-id` action, this will also use a very particular format
only expected to be used via the Singlewire administrative web application.

x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/sites/{siteId}/buildings"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'addressLine1': '8422 Old Sauk Road',
    'addressLine2': 'Suite 100',
    'city': 'Madison',
    'country': 'United States',
    'emergency': False,
    'name': 'Chipotle',
    'postalCode': '53717-4367',
    'primaryPhone': '+15558675309',
    'siteId': '2c9c6748-8519-11f1-a7f3-7dc13920e898',
    'state': 'Wisconsin'
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/supervisors"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'appEui': '0000000000000001',
    'appKey': 'ae4e4470e68a4fbd941b77ec0018cfba',
    'blePassword': 'be4e4470e68a4fbd941b77ec0018cfbb',
    'buildingId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-8faa-9f05022a0201',
    'eui': '0205f041c8e3e647',
    'floorId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-84d5-4a52e2fb2101',
    'isDecommissioned': True,
    'model': 'Moko_LW003B',
    'name': 'Primary iBeacon Supervisor',
    'pinnedLocation': '{:type "Point", :coordinates [-89.50275 43.114705]}',
    'salt': '8d6e1f0a2b3c4d5e',
    'siteId': '7e893e7c-c9f8-11ee-8be6-9f8e7d0fa83e',
    'zoneId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-8e64-4f8e4e3f1101'
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/tracking-events/{trackingEventId}/activities"

}

curl "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/tts" \
    -X POST \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
         "return": "hash",
         "subject": "Earl Grey tea, hot, is in the kitchen.",
         "ttsType": "subject",
         "ttsVoiceId": "751e56a1-841a-11ea-a32f-3767c65554ed"}'

post.description:
Typically, this is done in a single step, and the actual audio data is returned in response to the `POST`. If it is more convenient to separate the creation from the retrieval of the audio, the query parameter `return` can be passed with the value `hash`, and after creating or finding the audio, a description of it will be returned that includes the hash value that can be used with `GET` when the audio itself is desired.

Returns the audio of the synthesized speech, or the hash that can be used to obtain it (as described above), that results from the specified parameters.

For debugging purposes it is also possible to simulate a failure of text-to-speech by sending the value `fail` in the `return` query parameter. This will return the audio that reports that TTS failed.

get.description: Retrieves the list of all devices registered to a particular user. (The [Permissions](#tag--User-Permissions) and [Security Groups](#tag--Security-Groups) associated with the request, through the user attached to the access token, may limit the resources returned. A user will generally be able to see all devices they registered, but ordinary users will not be able to see devices registered by other users).

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user id path parameter

status.description: The response status code. Validation errors are indicated by 400.

message.description: A human-oriented text description of what went wrong.

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/users/{userId}/devices"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'build': 'Revision-1.5.77',
    'client': '00000000300BCE07',
    'clientIdentifier': '+1234567890',
    'deviceIdentifier': '+1234567890',
    'disabled': False,
    'name': 'Mobile',
    'os': 'iOS',
    'type': 'phone-call',
    'verified': True
}
try:
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

delete.description:
Deletes a notification from the list associated with a particular
user. When a user no longer wishes to see the notification in their
“in box” this method is the appropriate way to remove it. The fact
that the user was sent the notification will still be visible to
administrators through the [Notification Recipients](#tag--Notification-Recipients)
resource.

post.description:
Record an interaction between a [User](#tag--Users) and a [Notification](#tag--Notifications) they received. Currently, two different kinds of interaction can be reported, and they are differentiated by the value sent in the `type` parameter.
* `set-read-status` is an activity which marks the notification as read or unread as far as the user is concerned.
* `confirmation` records the user’s response to any [Confirmation Request](#tag--Confirmation-Requests) attached to the notification.

post.description:
Grants a user the permission to perform a specific action on a
specified resource or list of resources.

### Tied Extensions

There are two pairs of extensions which are now treated by the user
interface as a single concept: the iOS and Android push extensions are
jointly managed as InformaCast for iOS/Android, and the macOS and
Windows push extensions are jointly managed as InformaCast for
Mac/Windows. Because of this, granting a permission to one of either
pair will automatically grant permission to the other.

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException
import json

url = "https://api.icmobile.singlewire.com/api/v1/webex-calling-devices/{webexCallingDeviceId}"
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer S35QCVBN6S6TUQKHOND=',
    'Content-Type': 'application/json'
}
data = {
    'buildingId': '18e1f328-ffd0-4000-8faa-9f05022a0201',
    'floorId': 'fe1fe388-d23c-418d-b76d-46ce682c24ff',
    'ipAddress': '123.123.123.123',
    'name': 'The Bat Phone',
    'siteId': '7e893e7c-c9f8-11ee-8be6-9f8e7d0fa83e',
    'webexLocationId': 'Z2lzY29zcGFyazovL3VpURUFN',
    'zoneId': '68b84008-ba58-471e-aa29-e8acb725644c'
}
try:
    response = requests.put(url, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(data)).json()
    print(response)
except RequestException as e:
    print('Unexpected error!', e)

additionIds.description: The specific Device `icop_ids` that are in the Group regardless of filters and other Activation Groups. This is either a MAC Address (for speakers and idns) or the device identifier (for all other devices).

additions.description: The specific devices that are in the group regardless of filters and other activation groups.

defunct.description: Whether the device is defunct (meaning no Fusion server currently knows about it, but it remains assigned to one or more device groups).

description.description: The description of the device.

deviceIdentifier.description: Device type specific string used to identify the device

icopId.description: The id of the device used by the on-premises fusion server

isLicensed.description: Whether the device is currently licensed to be used.

type.description: The type of the device. Value can be `idns`, `speakers`, `plugins`, or `phones`

createdAt.description: The time the resource was created.

endpointIds.description: The endpoint(s) to which devices in this activation group should get sent.

endpoints.description: The endpoint(s) to which devices in this activation group should get sent.

exclusionIds.description: The specific device `icop_ids` that are excluded from the Activation Group regardless of filters and other Activation Groups. This is either a MAC Address (for speakers and idns) or the device identifier (for all other devices).

defunct.description: Whether the device is defunct (meaning no Fusion server currently knows about it, but it remains assigned to one or more device groups).

comparison.description: The comparison used for the filter (`BEGINS_WITH`, `CONTAINS`, `ENDS_WITH`, `EQUALS`, or `MATCHES_REGEX`; legal values depend on the attribute).

complement.description: Controls whether the filter’s meaning is reversed. If `true`, it will match devices that do not meet the specified criteria. (You might use this when it is simpler to specify what to exclude; you can think of it as adding “does not” in front of the rule.)

value.description: A text value for the comparison.

bellScheduleEntries.description: Bell schedule entries are the configured ring list for a particular day within a rolling schedule

entryNum.description: The day in a rolling schedule in which a ring list is applied. `entryNum` must be an integer between 0-20 which corresponds to a day in a rolling schedule. A rolling schedule can be one week (0-6), two weeks (0-13), or three weeks (0-20). Rolling schedules start on Monday, so 0 is the first Monday in the rolling schedule, 1 is the first Tuesday, etc.

ringListId.description: The `id` of the Ring List applied on `entryNum` day. If no rings are needed, this can be `null`.

ringListName.description: The name of the ring list applied on entryNum day. This is present in the response as a convenience, do not send it in a `PUT` or `POST`, it would be ignored.

bellScheduleExceptions.description: Bell schedule exceptions are overrides to one or more bell schedule entries attached to a bell schedule

description.description: Description of the exception to create in each bell schedule.
description.example: Snow Day

id.description: The id of the bell schedule exception, for efficient retrieval or manipulation of the exception. Not writable.

ringListId.description: The `id` of the Ring List that overrides the bell schedule entries ring list between the bell schedule exception’s `startDate` and `endDate`. If no rings are needed, this can be `null`.

ringListName.description: The name of the ring list overriding the ring list of the bell schedule entry. This is present in the response as a convenience, do not send it in a `PUT` or `POST`, it would be ignored.

createdAt.description: The time the resource was created.

description.description: An optional description for the bell schedule
description.example: School Year 2024-2025

endDate.description: The end date for your bell schedule in ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD format

isShared.description: Denotes whether or not this bell schedule is globally shared

name.description: A unique name for the bell schedule

numWeeks.description: The number of weeks in a bell schedule. Value can only be 1, 2, or 3 which corresponds to a weekly, bi-weekly, or tri-weekly schedule respectively.

startDate.description: The start date for your bell schedule in ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD format, must be on or after 2000-01-01

timeZone.description: The time zone of the ring list expression.

every.description: Used without `quota`, `first` or `rest`, to reply in the same way to all matching confirmation responses.

first.description: Can only be used when a `quota` and `rest` are specified. The text with which to immediately reply to the first `quota` respondents.

rest.description: Once `quota` number of respondents have confirmed with the `optionLabel` specified, we reply with the `rest` text to all subsequent confirmations of that `optionLabel` option.

_device-groups.filters.description: Defines rules to dynamically determine the Devices in this Device Group, combined as specified by filterType. If this Device Group is also filtered with baseDeviceGroupIds, these filters will further adjust the devices in the group.

caseSensitive.description: Controls whether comparisons should care about capitalization.

comparison.description: The comparison used for the filter (`BEGINS_WITH`, `CONTAINS`, `ENDS_WITH`, `EQUALS`, or `MATCHES_REGEX`; legal values depend on the attribute).

complement.description: Controls whether the filter’s meaning is reversed. If true, it will match devices that do not meet the specified criteria. (You might use this when it is simpler to specify what to exclude; you can think of it as adding “does not” in front of the rule.)

value.description: A text value for the comparison.

additionIds.description: The specific device icop_ids that are in the Group regardless of filters and other Device Groups. This is either the MAC Address (for speakers and idns) or the device identifier (for all other devices).

additions.description: The specific devices that are in the group regardless of filters and other device groups.

baseDeviceGroupIds.description: If present, contains a list of Device Group ids that form the starting Devices for this Device Group.

baseDeviceGroups.description: If present, contains the list of Device Group `id`s that form the starting Devices for this Device Group.

exclusionIds.description: The specific device icop_ids that are excluded from this device group regardless of filters and other device groups. This is either the MAC Address (for speakers and idns) or the device identifier (for all other devices).

exclusions.description: The specific devices that are excluded from this device group regardless of filters and other device groups.

facilityId.description: Facility ID associated with the device group.

filterType.description: Controls how the filters that define rules for dynamically determining the Devices in this Device Group are combined.  If this device group is also configured with baseDeviceGroupIds, the filters will further adjust the devices in the group.

filters.description: Defines rules to dynamically determine the Devices in this Device Group, combined as specified by filterType. If this Device Group is also filtered with baseDeviceGroupIds, these filters will further adjust the devices in the group.

name.description: The name of the cloned device group

numSpeakers.description: Number of matching speakers. Included if query contains the include-device-counts set to true.

Campaign

defaultNotificationDays.description: This number of days will be suggested to users on its self-service registration page as a good number of days to subscribe; they can override it if they desire. An empty value means the subscription never expires.

enrollmentEndDate.description: This value can be specified to establish a date after which self-service registrations will no longer be accepted. An empty value means the campaign is open-ended and will accept registrations indefinitely.

inactiveMessage.description: The text to display on the registration page after the enrollment end date to explain why registration is no longer available.

subscribeTag.description: Users will be able to join this campaign by sending this hashtag to: (608) 470-6356 (local; also valid internationally)

welcomeMessage.description: The text to display on its registration page to explain the purpose of the distribution list to members of the general public who would like to sign up to receive notifications.

numPlugins.description: Number of matching plugins. Included if query contains the include-device-counts set to true.

numSpeakers.description: Number of matching speakers. Included if query contains the include-device-counts set to true.

concatenatedText.description: Controls whether the short and long text will be concatenated together on the recipient’s display, the message icon will not be displayed, and no Details button will be offered

duration.description: Either the maximum time (in seconds) InformaCast will wait for recipient activation before beginning to play a message (when `synchronizedDelivery` is `true`), or the length of time for which InformaCast will play the message (default value `5`) when `synchronizedDelivery` is `false`

persistentText.description: Controls whether the text will persist on a phone’s display when the user exits a notification which arrives later

queueingAllowed.description: Allows ad-hoc messages to be broadcast in the order they were initiated, but without overlapping

repeatIndefinitely.description: Controls whether the message will be repeated until manually stopped. Must be consistent with `repetitions` if both are present

repetitionPause.description: The number of seconds to wait between repetitions of the message

repetitions.description: The number of times the message will repeat (setting `repetitions` to `0` means the message will repeat indefinitely). Must be consistent with `repeatIndefinitely` if both are present

ringtone.description: Sets whether a message will be available as a ring tone for the bell scheduler

scrollDuration.description: The number of seconds for which scrolling recipients will repeatedly move through a message (set either `scrollDuration` or `scrollRepetitions` but not both)

scrollRepetitions.description: The number of repetitions for which scrolling recipients will repeatedly move through a message (the value `0` means unlimited repetitions). Set either `scrollDuration` or `scrollRepetitions`, but not both; if both are `null`, three repetitions will be performed by scrolling recipients

scrollSpeed.description: The speed at which scrolling recipients will move through the message, a value ranging from `1` to `10`

skipPhonesInUse.description: Sets whether audio will be sent to phones on active calls (provided this can be determined)

skipUnresponsiveRecipients.description: Sets whether the message will be delivered only to recipients that are able to respond to it, i.e. phones and computers running the InformaCast Desktop Notifier

synchronizedDelivery.description: Controls whether InformaCast will wait until all devices are ready before starting to send the audio of the message

volume.description: The volume at which to play the message

customVars.description: Key/value pairs which can replace placeholders in the message template when the notification is created. The placeholder syntax in message templates will be of the form `{{varName}}`, where `varName` was one of the keys supplied here, and the corresponding value will be substituted into the notification.

deviceGroupIds.description: A list of the `id`s of all device groups to which this notification was sent

deviceGroups.description: The device groups, if any, to which this notification was sent

distributionListIds.description: A list of the `id`s of all distribution lists to which this notification was sent

distributionLists.description: The distribution lists, if any, to which this notification was sent

draft.description: If omitted, an actual notification is sent. If supplied with the value `true`, a notification preview is created, showing who would have received it if it were actually sent, but no actual sending will occur. As a query filter can be set to `true` to exclusively list this user's draft notifications.

expiresAt.description: When this notification will (or did) expire, can be `null` for non-expiring notifications

extensionSettingsOverrides.description: Allows device extensions which were enabled in the notification profile to be disabled at send time: an object whose keys are device extension `id`s, and values are `{"disabled": true}`

facilityId.description: The ID of the facility this notification belongs to

followUpAllowed.description: Denotes whether this notification can be the source of a follow-up notification (using `followUpForNotificationId`); client applications can use this to enable buttons in a user interface

followUpForNotificationId.description: The `id` of the source notification for which a follow-up message is being sent (and which will define the recipients; `followUpAllowed` must be `true` for that notification). This value can refer to any eligible notification in the history and must be paired with `followUpForNotificationQuery` for recipient selection. The `messageTemplateId` provided must support follow-up (i.e. its `followUp` value must be true)

followUpForNotificationQuery.description: The query used at send time to create the list of recipients based on the `followUpForNotificationId` also supplied; the value uses the same search query syntax used for `q` filters (i.e. `read:true` would extract all recipients that read the original notification); recipients determined from this query are merged with any configured on the message template

image.description: Length of the attached image, in bytes, or zero if there is none

imageAltText.description: Optional description of image that may be read by assistive technology

imageFiles.description: Lists information about the image files sent with this notification. Currently there will only be one, but future releases may support multiple images with different purposes or quality

imageMimeType.description: The format in which the image data will be delivered, if there is an image associated with this notification (this is now redundant with the `imageFiles` attribute, and will likely be deprecated and removed in a future release)

incidentId.description: The `id` of the incident this notification started or belongs to

incidentPlanId.description:
The `id` of the incident plan, if any, used to create an incident when sending this notification
_Accessing this feature requires 'Respond'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

The type of user. Application users are for non-interactive use with permanent API tokens, such as with the InformaCast plugin; anonymous users cannot be created via the API, but are created when users register a phone anonymously to participate in a campaign. Support users also cannot be created via the API.

_Specifying value 'rostered' requires 'Reach'._
_Specifying value 'application' requires 'Reach'._

_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

Message templates that support a follow-up are subject to different validation rules (i.e. all recipient fields can be set non-customizable in addition to specifying no recipients when saving the template)

customVars.description: Key/value pairs which can replace placeholders in the message template when the notification is created. The placeholder syntax in message templates will be of the form `{{varName}}`, where `varName` was one of the keys supplied here, and the corresponding value will be substituted into the notification.

draft.description: If omitted, an actual notification is sent. If supplied with the value `true`, a notification preview is created, showing who would have received it if it were actually sent, but no actual sending will occur. As a query filter can be set to `true` to exclusively list this user's draft notifications.

followUpForNotificationId.description: The `id` of the source notification for which a follow-up message is being sent (and which will define the recipients; `followUpAllowed` must be `true` for that notification). This value can refer to any eligible notification in the history and must be paired with `followUpForNotificationQuery` for recipient selection. The `messageTemplateId` provided must support follow-up (i.e. its `followUp` value must be true)

followUpForNotificationQuery.description: The query used at send time to create the list of recipients based on the `followUpForNotificationId` also supplied; the value uses the same search query syntax used for `q` filters (i.e. `read:true` would extract all recipients that read the original notification); recipients determined from this query are merged with any configured on the message template

axlAddress.description: A comma-delimited list of IP Addresses that InformaCast Fusion will try when contacting the Administrative XML (AXL) service. These will always be tried in priority order, from left to right.

axlPassword.description: The password with which InformaCast Fusion will attempt to contact the Administrative XML (AXL) service.

axlUser.description: The username with which InformaCast Fusion will attempt to contact the Administrative XML (AXL) service. Used to discover and configure phones.

capfAddress.description: Only used when `secureConnection` is `true`. The address of the Certificate Authority Proxy Function (CAPF) Service, used for generating certificates.

capfPort.description: Only used when `secureConnection` is `true`. The port of the Certificate Authority Proxy Function (CAPF) Service, used for generating certificates.

nextAuthenticationUrl.description: If InformaCast Fusion determines that it should not attempt to handle a phone authentication request, it will redirect those authentication requests to this location

secureConnection.description: Specifies whether a secure CTI connection will be established with the Cisco Unified Communications Manager cluster, i.e. `true`=secure CTI connection, `false`=non-secure CTI connection. Secure CTI connections require Cisco Unified CM 12.5(1)SU7 or 14SU2 and later running in mixed mode.

snmpAuthPassword.description: Only used with SNMP Version 3. The authentication password that was created in CUCM for InformaCast Fusion. SNMP is used to obtain the IP addresses of registered phones when building the phone cache.

snmpCommunity.description: Only used with SNMP Version 2. The community name that was created in CUCM for InformaCast Fusion. SNMP is used to obtain the IP addresses of registered phones when building the phone cache.

snmpEncryptPassword.description: Only used with SNMP Version 3. The privacy password that was created in CUCM for InformaCast Fusion. SNMP is used to obtain the IP addresses of registered phones when building the phone cache.

snmpUsername.description: Only used with SNMP Version 3. The username that was created in CUCM for InformaCast Fusion. SNMP is used to obtain the IP addresses of registered phones when building the phone cache.

snmpVersion.description: Specifies the version of SNMP InformaCast Fusion will use when discovering phones. Valid values are `2` or `3`. If `2`, then only `snmpCommunity` is required. If `3`, then only `snmpUsername`, `snmpAuthPassword`, and `snmpEncryptPassword` are required.
