allowRestartForJtapiUpdate.description: If `true`, will allow this operation even if the JTAPI version of the CUCM Cluster and Fusion Server do not match, which will restart the Fusion Server automatically when saved. Otherwise, throws a validation error if they do not match.

cmAddress.description: A comma-delimited list of IP Addresses used to connect to the CUCM CTIManager service; these will always be tried in priority order, from left to right

cmPassword.description: The password used to connect to the CUCM CTIManager service; used when JTAPI enabled only

cmUsername.description: The username used to connect to the CUCM CTIManager service; used when JTAPI enabled only.

endpointId.description: The Fusion Server ID with which this configuration should be associated

jtapiPhoneActivationEnabled.description: Only used when `makeCtiConnection` is `true`. If `true`, phones are activated via JTAPI requests sent by InformaCast to the primary CUCM cluster. If `false`, phones are activated via HTTP requests sent from InformaCast to IP phones.

jtapiStaticTerminalsEnabled.description: Only used when `jtapiPhoneActivationEnabled` is `true`. If `true`, will eagerly create telephony terminals for all phones associated with the cluster and assigned to this Fusion Server by Activation Group, speeding up activation time. If `false`, terminals are created during activation and discarded afterward.

makeCtiConnection.description: Allows CTI connections to this CUCM cluster and makes this CUCM cluster the "Primary" cluster for this Fusion Server. A Fusion Server may have at most 1 Primary Cluster. Setting this value to true will unset it for any other CUCM cluster assigned to this Fusion Server.

additionIds.description: The specific device icop_ids that are in the Group regardless of filters and other Device Groups. This is either the MAC Address (for speakers and idns) or the device identifier (for all other devices).

additions.description: The specific devices that are in the group regardless of filters and other device groups.

baseDeviceGroupIds.description: If present, contains a list of Device Group ids that form the starting Devices for this Device Group.

baseDeviceGroups.description: If present, contains the list of Device Group `id`s that form the starting Devices for this Device Group.

exclusionIds.description: The specific device icop_ids that are excluded from this device group regardless of filters and other device groups. This is either the MAC Address (for speakers and idns) or the device identifier (for all other devices).

exclusions.description: The specific devices that are excluded from this device group regardless of filters and other device groups.

facilityId.description: Facility ID associated with the device group.

filterType.description: Controls how the filters that define rules for dynamically determining the Devices in this Device Group are combined.  If this device group is also configured with baseDeviceGroupIds, the filters will further adjust the devices in the group.

filters.description: Defines rules to dynamically determine the Devices in this Device Group, combined as specified by filterType. If this Device Group is also filtered with baseDeviceGroupIds, these filters will further adjust the devices in the group.

id.description: The `id` of this specific device group, allowing it to be manipulated or retrieved individually.

isShared.description: Denotes whether or not this device group is globally shared

logicalExpression.description: Specify a combination of rules based on the index of the rule and using logical operators 'and' and 'or' along with parentheses. Used when filterType is 'LOGICAL_EXPRESSION

numIdns.description: Number of matching IDNs. Included if query contains the include-device-counts set to true.

numPhones.description: Number of matching phones. Included if query contains the include-device-counts set to true.

campaign.description:
Campaign
_Accessing this feature requires 'Reach'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

defaultNotificationDays.description: This number of days will be suggested to users on its self-service registration page as a good number of days to subscribe; they can override it if they desire. An empty value means the subscription never expires.

enrollmentEndDate.description: This value can be specified to establish a date after which self-service registrations will no longer be accepted. An empty value means the campaign is open-ended and will accept registrations indefinitely.

inactiveMessage.description: The text to display on the registration page after the enrollment end date to explain why registration is no longer available.

subscribeTag.description: Users will be able to join this campaign by sending this hashtag to: (608) 470-6356 (local; also valid internationally)

welcomeMessage.description: The text to display on its registration page to explain the purpose of the distribution list to members of the general public who would like to sign up to receive notifications.
welcomeMessage.example: Welcome to the World Barista Championship

isShared.description: Denotes whether or not this distribution list is globally shared

isSubscribable.description: Marks the distribution list as subscribable for self service

This value must be consistent with the `capture-keepalive-seconds` parameter in the `InformaCastSpeaker.cfg` file, and all instances of the Singlewire Desktop Notification System must use the same interval

speakerDefaultVolume.description: Automatically added hardware IP speaker volume

speakerRegistrationInterval.description: The interval at which all types of IP speakers register with InformaCast (the default is 300 seconds). This value must be consistent with the `registration-interval` parameter defined in the `InformaCastSpeaker.cfg` file, and all IP speaker types must use the same interval. This field's value is in seconds, while the value in `InformaCastSpeaker.cfg` is in minutes. So this field's value must be 60 times the value in the `InformaCastSpeaker.cfg` file

removeExpiredDesktopAfterDays.description: The number of days in which configured and unregistered IDN instances will be automatically removed. A value of `0` implies no IDNs are ever removed

alertTone.description: Controls the sound played by a mobile phone or other compatible InformaCast Fusion Devices (i.e. desktops) when this message template is sent; `default` means to play the standard alert tone chosen by the device’s owner, `silent` means to play no tone, and other values choose specific named sounds

alertToneCustomizable.description: Whether `alertTone` can be customized when the notification is sent (defaults to `false`)

areaOfInterestIds.description: The `id`s of the Areas of Interest to which notifications based on this template should be sent

areaOfInterestIdsCustomizable.description: Whether `areaOfInterestIds` can be customized when the notification is sent (defaults to `false`)

areaOfInterestIdsDisplay.description: Informs client applications whether the area of interest section should be displayed to senders when composing a notification based on this template, defaults to `show` when `areaOfInterestIds` are supplied

audio.description: Default audio content, a binary stream of audio data. The audio formats accepted include μ-law, Opus, MP3, Vorbis, or some other common WAV formats. Non μ-law files will be converted to μ-law and the size of the converted audio must not exceed [10 MiB](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Byte#Multiple-byte_units) In responses, the size in bytes of the attached audio, or zero if there is none (this is now redundant with the `audioFiles` attribute, and will likely be deprecated and removed in a future release)

audioCustomizable.description: Whether `audio` can be customized when the notification is sent (defaults to `false`)

audioDisplay.description: Informs client applications whether the audio section should be displayed to senders when composing a notification based on this template, defaults to `show` when audio is present

body.description: Default optional detailed body text, may be up to 10,000 characters long
body.example: Please stay alert for updates as we track the intruder.

bodyCustomizable.description: Whether the body can be customized when the notification is sent (defaults to `false`)

cancelNotificationsType.description: When a notification sent with this message template, determines what other notifications should be canceled: `none`, `all`, or `specific` (based on the message templates used for those other notifications)

cancelNotificationsTypeDisplay.description: Informs client applications whether the cancellation section should be displayed to senders when composing a notification based on this template

expirationDisplay.description: Informs client applications whether the expiration section should be displayed to senders when composing a notification based on this template, defaults to `show` when expiration is present

followUp.description: Denotes whether this template is usable for a follow-up: if set to `true`, this template can be used as the `messageTemplateId` when sending a new notification based on an existing notification in the notification history. If set to `false`, attempting to use this template in the same manner will result in an error. Message templates that support a follow-up are subject to different validation rules (i.e. all recipient fields can be set non-customizable in addition to specifying no recipients when saving the template)

image.description: Binary stream of the default image to send with the notification. The size of the image must not exceed [10 MiB](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Byte#Multiple-byte_units). In responses, the size in bytes of the attached image, or zero if there is none (this is now redundant with the `imageFiles` attribute, and will likely be deprecated and removed in a future release)

imageAltText.description: Optional description of image that may be read by assistive technology

imageCustomizable.description: Whether `image` can be customized when the notification is sent (defaults to `false`)

imageDisplay.description: Informs client applications whether the image section should be displayed to senders when composing a notification based on this template, defaults to `show` when image is present

imageMimeType.description: The content type of the default image attached to the message template, if there is one (this is now redundant with the `imageFiles` attribute, and will likely be deprecated and removed in a future release)

isShared.description: Denotes whether or not this notification profile is globally shared

name.description: The notification profile name, may be up to 140 characters long

settings.description: An array of notification profile settings objects

isShared.description: Denotes whether or not this notification profile is globally shared

name.description: The notification profile name, may be up to 140 characters long

settings.description: An array of notification profile settings objects

attachments.description: Binary attachments (see the [Attachments section of Notification Profiles](https://openapi.icmobile.singlewire.com/#tag--Notification-Profiles) for how these are uploaded)

disabled.description: If `true`, exclude this device type from notifications.

ipSpeakerAssets.description: Binary attachments (see the [IP speaker assets section of Notification Profiles](https://openapi.icmobile.singlewire.com/#tag--Notification-Profiles) for how these are uploaded)

value.description: Actual notification settings for this extension

additionalDeviceRecipients.description: An array of additional devices that should receive this notification.

alertTone.description: The alert tone to use for mobile push notifications

areaOfInterestIds.description: A list of the `id`s of all areas of interest to which this notification was sent

audio.description: Binary stream of audio to send with the notification. The audio formats accepted include μ-law, Opus, MP3, Vorbis, or some other common WAV formats. Non μ-law files will be converted to μ-law and the size of the converted audio must not exceed 10 [MiB](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mebibyte). In responses, the size in bytes of the audio sent with this notification, or zero if there is none (this is now redundant in the response with the `audioFiles` attribute, and will likely be deprecated and removed from the response in a future release)

body.description: Optional detailed body text for the notification, up to 10,000 characters long
body.example: Warning: An intruder has been spotted on campus heading towards the recreation center. Please be advised and stay tuned for further updates. Have you seen the intruder?

collaborationGroupIds.description:
A list of the `id`s of all outbound systems to which this notification was sent
_Accessing this feature requires 'Reach'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

confirmationRequestId.description:
The `id` of the confirmation request attached to the notification, if any
_Accessing this feature requires 'Respond'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

customVars.description: Key/value pairs which can replace placeholders in the message template when the notification is created. The placeholder syntax in message templates will be of the form `{{varName}}`, where `varName` was one of the keys supplied here, and the corresponding value will be substituted into the notification.

deviceGroupIds.description: A list of the `id`s of all device groups to which this notification was sent

distributionListIds.description: A list of the `id`s of all distribution lists to which this notification was sent

draft.description: If omitted, an actual notification is sent. If supplied with the value `true`, a notification preview is created, showing who would have received it if it were actually sent, but no actual sending will occur. As a query filter can be set to `true` to exclusively list this user's draft notifications.

expiresAt.description: When this notification will (or did) expire, can be `null` for non-expiring notifications

extensionSettingsOverrides.description: Allows device extensions which were enabled in the notification profile to be disabled at send time: an object whose keys are device extension `id`s, and values are `{"disabled": true}`

followUpForNotificationId.description: The `id` of the source notification for which a follow-up message is being sent (and which will define the recipients; `followUpAllowed` must be `true` for that notification). This value can refer to any eligible notification in the history and must be paired with `followUpForNotificationQuery` for recipient selection. The `messageTemplateId` provided must support follow-up (i.e. its `followUp` value must be true)

followUpForNotificationQuery.description: The query used at send time to create the list of recipients based on the `followUpForNotificationId` also supplied; the value uses the same search query syntax used for `q` filters (i.e. `read:true` would extract all recipients that read the original notification); recipients determined from this query are merged with any configured on the message template

image.description: Binary stream of an image to send with the notification, size must not exceed 10 [MiB](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mebibyte). In responses, the size in bytes of the image sent with this notification, or zero if there is none (this is now redundant in the response with the `imageFiles` attribute, and will likely be deprecated and removed from the response in a future release)

imageAltText.description: Optional description of image that may be read by assistive technology

imageMimeType.description: The format in which the image data will be delivered, if there is an image associated with this notification (this is now redundant with the `imageFiles` attribute, and will likely be deprecated and removed in a future release)

incidentPlanId.description:
The `id` of the incident plan, if any, used to create an incident when sending this notification
_Accessing this feature requires 'Respond'._
_Contact Singlewire sales for more information about Modules._

messageTemplateId.description: The `id` of the Message Template that serves as the basis for this notification: provides default values for the notification attributes and controls whether they can be overridden at send time. This field is required to send a notification.

metadata.description: Optional values to be attached to the notification, for use by client software

notificationSource.description: The source of the notification. This field is populated on notification creation and is the recommended attribute for filtering.

priority.description: The priority level of this notification. If the priority level is critical, will send a notification bypassing a device's 'Do not Disturb' settings.

subject.description: Subject (perhaps a summary version) of the notification, up to 255 characters long
subject.example: An intruder has been spotted on campus

ttsCustomContent.description: Custom text used for synthesized speech when `ttsType` is `custom-text` or `custom-ssml`

ttsSpeed.description: The rate at which the text was spoken (defaults to `normal` if tts is in use)

ttsType.description: Controls which attributes were transformed into speech audio, and how

ttsVoiceId.description: Records the Text-to-Speech Voice, if any, used when synthesizing audio for this notification

userIds.description: A list of the `id`s of individual users to which this notification was sent, regardless of their distribution list memberships

validateTts.description: Controls what validation to perform on the TTS text; the default is `none`

callbackNumber.description: An optional callback number that mobile panic buttons will supply for our emergency service provider

event.description: For integration scenarios, the raw event from the microservice

fields.description: Provides the answers to the scenario's fields. More information can be found [here](#tag--Scenario-Notifications)

initiationDevice.description: Details about the device that initiated the scenario. This value is stored in the metadata field of the `initiationDevice` in the response

lookupValue.description: The value used to identify this device in InformaCast. For API Devices, this is the External ID. For IP Speakers, it's the MAC Address.

type.description: The type of the device, either api-device or ip-speaker

location.description: For site-enabled scenarios, and integration scenarios with dynamic sites, the site, building, floor, and zone info

locationResolutionStrategy.description: Sets how the final reported location of the scenario and site roles are determined. Items are checked in the order present and only items present are considered. Defaults to ["location", "device"].

messages.description: The array of messages associated with a scenario. More information can be found [here](#tag--Scenario-Notifications)

backgroundColor.description: The scenario’s background color. Should be a hexadecimal color value lead with a #.

confirmationPin.description: Numeric pin user must enter to initiate scenario when `confirmationType` is `pin`.

confirmationType.description: Informs the user interface how and if to require confirmation before initiating the scenario

defaultBuildingId.description: For location-enabled scenarios, the building to use when sending the scenario in a way which does not allow the user to be prompted for a location

defaultFloorId.description: For location-enabled scenarios, the floor to use when sending the scenario in a way which does not allow the user to be prompted for a location

defaultSiteId.description: For location-enabled scenarios, the site to use when sending the scenario in a way which does not allow the user to be prompted for a location

defaultZoneId.description: For location-enabled scenarios, the zone to use when sending the scenario in a way which does not allow the user to be prompted for a location
